source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the API server pod specification file ownership is set to root:root
description: Ensure	that	the	API	server	pod	specification	file	ownership	is	set	to	root:root.
rationale: The	API	server	pod	specification	file	controls	various	parameters	that	set	the	behavior	of the	API	server.	You	should	set	its	file	ownership	to	maintain	the	integrity	of	the	file.	The	file should	be	owned	by	root:root. Impact: None
audit: Run	the	below	command	(based	on	the	file	location	on	your	system)	on	the	master	node. For	example, stat -c %U:%G /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml Verify	that	the	ownership	is	set	to	root:root.
remediation: Run	the	below	command	(based	on	the	file	location	on	your	system)	on	the	master	node. For	example, chown root:root /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml Default	Value: By	default,	the	kube-apiserver.yaml	file	ownership	is	set	to	root:root. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 18	|	P a g e Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 5.1	Minimize	And	Sparingly	Use	Administrative	Privileges Minimize	administrative	privileges	and	only	use	administrative	accounts	when	they	are required.	Implement	focused	auditing	on	the	use	of	administrative	privileged	functions	and monitor	for	anomalous	behavior. Version	7 5.1	Establish	Secure	Configurations Maintain	documented,	standard	security	configuration	standards	for	all	authorized operating	systems	and	software. 19	|	P a g e
references: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 18	|	P a g e Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 5.1	Minimize	And	Sparingly	Use	Administrative	Privileges Minimize	administrative	privileges	and	only	use	administrative	accounts	when	they	are required.	Implement	focused	auditing	on	the	use	of	administrative	privileged	functions	and monitor	for	anomalous	behavior. Version	7 5.1	Establish	Secure	Configurations Maintain	documented,	standard	security	configuration	standards	for	all	authorized operating	systems	and	software. 19	|	P a g e
justification: Both AI opinions correctly identify that this control requires verification of file ownership at the OS level on the Kubernetes master node, specifically for the `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` file. Alibaba Cloud APIs, CLI, or managed services (ACK) do not expose file-level metadata such as ownership or permissions, nor do they allow direct inspection of node filesystems for managed clusters. The CIS benchmark itself explicitly states this control cannot be audited or modified in ACK managed clusters, confirming that only manual inspection is possible.
manual_summary: 1. **Access the Master Node:** - Securely SSH into the master node of your Kubernetes cluster where the API server is running. Ensure SSH access is logged and uses key-based authentication. 2. **Locate the Pod Specification File:** - Identify the location of the API server pod specification file, typically `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml`. 3. **Verify File Ownership:** - Run the following command: `stat -c %U:%G /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` 4. **Check the Output:** - Confirm that the output is `root:root`, indicating the file is owned by the `root` user and group. 5. **Expected Secure Configuration:** - The file must be owned by `root:root` to maintain integrity and prevent unauthorized changes. 6. **Confirm Compliance:** - If the file is not owned by `root:root`, remediate by running: `chown root:root /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` - Document the check and remediation for audit purposes.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the controller manager pod specification file permissions are set to 644 or more restrictive
description: Ensure	that	the	controller	manager	pod	specification	file	has	permissions	of	644	or	more restrictive.
rationale: The	controller	manager	pod	specification	file	controls	various	parameters	that	set	the behavior	of	the	Controller	Manager	on	the	master	node.	You	should	restrict	its	file permissions	to	maintain	the	integrity	of	the	file.	The	file	should	be	writable	by	only	the administrators	on	the	system. Impact: None
audit: Run	the	below	command	(based	on	the	file	location	on	your	system)	on	the	master	node. For	example, stat -c %a /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-controller-manager.yaml Verify	that	the	permissions	are	644	or	more	restrictive.
remediation: Run	the	below	command	(based	on	the	file	location	on	your	system)	on	the	master	node. For	example, chmod 644 /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-controller-manager.yaml Default	Value: By	default,	the	kube-controller-manager.yaml	file	has	permissions	of	640. References: 20	|	P a g e 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 5.1	Minimize	And	Sparingly	Use	Administrative	Privileges Minimize	administrative	privileges	and	only	use	administrative	accounts	when	they	are required.	Implement	focused	auditing	on	the	use	of	administrative	privileged	functions	and monitor	for	anomalous	behavior. Version	7 5.1	Establish	Secure	Configurations Maintain	documented,	standard	security	configuration	standards	for	all	authorized operating	systems	and	software. 21	|	P a g e
references: 20	|	P a g e 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 5.1	Minimize	And	Sparingly	Use	Administrative	Privileges Minimize	administrative	privileges	and	only	use	administrative	accounts	when	they	are required.	Implement	focused	auditing	on	the	use	of	administrative	privileged	functions	and monitor	for	anomalous	behavior. Version	7 5.1	Establish	Secure	Configurations Maintain	documented,	standard	security	configuration	standards	for	all	authorized operating	systems	and	software. 21	|	P a g e
justification: Both AI opinions correctly identify that this control requires checking file permissions on the underlying master node filesystem, which is not accessible via Alibaba Cloud APIs, CLI, or managed service interfaces. Alibaba Cloud ACK managed clusters do not expose OS-level file details or allow direct access to pod specification files through any automated means. Therefore, this control can only be audited manually by connecting to the master node and inspecting the file permissions directly.
manual_summary: 1. **Connect to the Master Node:** Use SSH to access the master node where the Kubernetes controller manager is running. 2. **Locate the Pod Specification File:** Identify the path to the controller manager pod specification file (commonly `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-controller-manager.yaml`). 3. **Check File Permissions:** Run the command: `stat -c %a /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-controller-manager.yaml` 4. **Verify Permissions:** Ensure the output is `644` or a more restrictive value (e.g., `640`, `600`). 5. **Remediate if Necessary:** If permissions are not compliant, set them with: `chmod 644 /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-controller-manager.yaml` 6. **Confirm Compliance:** Re-run the `stat` command to verify the permissions are now correct.

title: Ensure that the etcd data directory permissions are set to 700 or more restrictive
description: Ensure	that	the	etcd	data	directory	has	permissions	of	700	or	more	restrictive.
rationale: etcd	is	a	highly-available	key-value	store	used	by	Kubernetes	deployments	for	persistent storage	of	all	of	its	REST	API	objects.	This	data	directory	should	be	protected	from	any unauthorized	reads	or	writes.	It	should	not	be	readable	or	writable	by	any	group	members or	the	world. Impact: None
audit: On	the	etcd	server	node,	get	the	etcd	data	directory,	passed	as	an	argument	--data-dir, from	the	below	command: ps -ef | grep etcd Run	the	below	command	(based	on	the	etcd	data	directory	found	above).	For	example, stat -c %a /var/lib/etcd/data.etcd Verify	that	the	permissions	are	700	or	more	restrictive.
remediation: On	the	etcd	server	node,	get	the	etcd	data	directory,	passed	as	an	argument	--data-dir, from	the	below	command: ps -ef | grep etcd Run	the	below	command	(based	on	the	etcd	data	directory	found	above).	For	example, chmod 700 /var/lib/etcd/data.etcd 36	|	P a g e Default	Value: By	default,	etcd	data	directory	has	permissions	of	755. references: 1. https://coreos.com/etcd/docs/latest/op-guide/configuration.html#data-dir 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/etcd/ Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 14	Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Version	7 14	Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know 37	|	P a g e
justification: The audit requires checking and potentially modifying the file system permissions of the etcd data directory on the underlying OS of the etcd server node. This information is not exposed via Alibaba Cloud APIs, CLI, or Config rules, nor is it available through ACK managed cluster interfaces. While Alibaba Cloud Cloud Assistant could be used to script the check on ECS instances, it still fundamentally requires OS-level access and is not a native cloud API/SDK/Config-based control. Therefore, this control must be audited manually.
manual_summary: 1. **Access the etcd Server Node:** SSH into the server (ECS instance or bare metal) where the etcd process is running. 2. **Identify the etcd Data Directory:** Run `ps -ef | grep etcd` to locate the etcd process and find the `--data-dir` argument, which specifies the etcd data directory path. 3. **Check Directory Permissions:** Execute `stat -c %a /path/to/etcd/data/dir` (replace with the actual path found above) to display the directory permissions. 4. **Verify Compliance:** Confirm that the permissions are set to `700` or more restrictive (i.e., only the owner has read, write, and execute permissions). 5. **Expected Secure Configuration:** The directory permissions must be `700` or more restrictive. If not, remediate with `chmod 700 /path/to/etcd/data/dir`. 6. **Document Results:** Record the output and compliance status for audit evidence.

manual_summary: 1. SSH into the Kubernetes master node of your ACK cluster. 2. Run the command: `ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver` to list the running arguments of the kube-apiserver process. 3. Examine the output and confirm that the `--token-auth-file` parameter is NOT present. 4. If the parameter is found, edit `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` to remove the `--token-auth-file=<filename>` argument, then restart the kube-apiserver. 5. Confirm compliance by repeating step 2 and ensuring the parameter is absent.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the --kubelet-https argument is set to true

audit: Run	the	following	command	on	the	master	node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify	that	the	--kubelet-https	argument	either	does	not	exist	or	is	set	to	true.
remediation: Edit	the	API	server	pod	specification	file	/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml	on	the	master	node	and	remove	the	--kubelet-https	parameter. Default	Value: By	default,	kubelet	connections	are	over	https. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kubelet-authentication-authorization/ Additional	Information: 62	|	P a g e This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 14.2	Encrypt	All	Sensitive	Information	Over	Less-trusted	Networks All	communication	of	sensitive	information	over	less-trusted	networks	should	be encrypted.	Whenever	information	flows	over	a	network	with	a	lower	trust	level,	the information	should	be	encrypted. Version	7 14.4	Encrypt	All	Sensitive	Information	in	Transit Encrypt	all	sensitive	information	in	transit. 63	|	P a g e
impact: You	require	TLS	to	be	configured	on	apiserver	as	well	as	kubelets.

CIS	Controls: Version	6 14.2	Encrypt	All	Sensitive	Information	Over	Less-trusted	Networks All	communication	of	sensitive	information	over	less-trusted	networks	should	be encrypted.	Whenever	information	flows	over	a	network	with	a	lower	trust	level,	the information	should	be	encrypted. justification: Direct verification of the `--kubelet-https` argument requires access to the kube-apiserver process or its manifest file on the master node. manual_summary: 1. **Access the Kubernetes Master Node:** SSH into the master node of your Kubernetes cluster using appropriate credentials. 2. **Check the kube-apiserver Process:** Run the command: `ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver` 3. **Verify the Argument:** In the output, locate the `--kubelet-https` argument. - If the argument is present, ensure it is set to `true` (i.e., `--kubelet-https=true`). - If the argument is absent, the default is `true` (secure). 4. **Confirm Compliance:** Compliance is achieved if `--kubelet-https` is either not present (default secure) or explicitly set to `true`. 5. **Document Findings:** Record the command output and your verification for audit purposes.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the --kubelet-client-certificate and --kubelet-client-key arguments are set as appropriate
description: Enable	certificate	based	kubelet	authentication.
rationale: The	apiserver,	by	default,	does	not	authenticate	itself	to	the	kubelet's	HTTPS	endpoints. The	requests	from	the	apiserver	are	treated	anonymously.	You	should	set	up	certificate- based	kubelet	authentication	to	ensure	that	the	apiserver	authenticates	itself	to	kubelets when	submitting	requests. Impact: You	require	TLS	to	be	configured	on	apiserver	as	well	as	kubelets.
audit: Run	the	following	command	on	the	master	node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify	that	the	--kubelet-client-certificate	and	--kubelet-client-key	arguments exist	and	they	are	set	as	appropriate.
remediation: Follow	the	Kubernetes	documentation	and	set	up	the	TLS	connection	between	the apiserver	and	kubelets.	Then,	edit	API	server	pod	specification	file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml	on	the	master	node	and	set	the kubelet	client	certificate	and	key	parameters	as	below. --kubelet-client-certificate=<path/to/client-certificate-file> --kubelet-client-key=<path/to/client-key-file> Default	Value: By	default,	certificate-based	kubelet	authentication	is	not	set. 64	|	P a g e References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kubelet-authentication-authorization/ 3. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/master-node- communication/#apiserver---kubelet Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 3.4	Use	Only	Secure	Channels	For	Remote	System	Administration Perform	all	remote	administration	of	servers,	workstation,	network	devices,	and	similar equipment	over	secure	channels.	Protocols	such	as	telnet,	VNC,	RDP,	or	others	that	do	not actively	support	strong	encryption	should	only	be	used	if	they	are	performed	over	a secondary	encryption	channel,	such	as	SSL,	TLS	or	IPSEC. Version	7 4.5	Use	Multifactor	Authentication	For	All	Administrative	Access Use	multi-factor	authentication	and	encrypted	channels	for	all	administrative	account access. 65	|	P a g e
impact: You	require	TLS	to	be	configured	on	apiserver	as	well	as	kubelets.

Protocols	such	as	telnet,	VNC,	RDP,	or	others	that	do	not actively	support	strong	encryption	should	only	be	used	if	they	are	performed	over	a secondary	encryption	channel,	such	as	SSL,	TLS	or	IPSEC. Version	7 4.5	Use	Multifactor	Authentication	For	All	Administrative	Access Use	multi-factor	authentication	and	encrypted	channels	for	all	administrative	account access. 65	|	P a g e
justification: Both AI opinions correctly identify that the required audit—verifying the presence and correctness of the --kubelet-client-certificate and --kubelet-client-key arguments in the kube-apiserver process—requires direct access to the master node’s configuration or process arguments. Alibaba Cloud’s managed ACK service does not expose these settings via any API, CLI, or console interface, nor are they available through Alibaba Cloud Config or other automation tools. Therefore, this check cannot be performed automatically and must be conducted manually.
manual_summary: 1. **SSH into the Master Node:** Obtain SSH access to the Kubernetes master node. This typically requires administrator credentials and may not be possible in Alibaba Cloud managed ACK clusters. 2. **List kube-apiserver Process Arguments:** Run the following command to display the running kube-apiserver process and its arguments: ```bash ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver ``` 3. **Verify Certificate Arguments:** Inspect the command output and confirm that both `--kubelet-client-certificate` and `--kubelet-client-key` arguments are present and point to valid certificate and key file paths. 4. **Confirm Secure Configuration:** Ensure the certificate and key files referenced are valid, exist on disk, and are appropriately secured. 5. **Document Findings:** Record whether both arguments are set and valid. If not, document the absence or misconfiguration for remediation.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the --authorization-mode argument is not set to AlwaysAllow

rationale: The	API	Server,	can	be	configured	to	allow	all	requests.	This	mode	should	not	be	used	on any	production	cluster. Impact: Only	authorized	requests	will	be	served.

One	such	example	could	be	as	below. --authorization-mode=RBAC Default	Value: By	default,	AlwaysAllow	is	not	enabled. title: Ensure that the admission control plugin EventRateLimit is set
description: Limit	the	rate	at	which	the	API	server	accepts	requests.
rationale: Using	EventRateLimit	admission	control	enforces	a	limit	on	the	number	of	events	that	the API	Server	will	accept	in	a	given	time	slice.	A	misbehaving	workload	could	overwhelm	and DoS	the	API	Server,	making	it	unavailable.	This	particularly	applies	to	a	multi-tenant cluster,	where	there	might	be	a	small	percentage	of	misbehaving	tenants	which	could	have a	significant	impact	on	the	performance	of	the	cluster	overall.	Hence,	it	is	recommended	to limit	the	rate	of	events	that	the	API	server	will	accept. Note:	This	is	an	Alpha	feature	in	the	Kubernetes	1.15	release. Impact: You	need	to	carefully	tune	in	limits	as	per	your	environment.
audit: Run	the	following	command	on	the	master	node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify	that	the	--enable-admission-plugins	argument	is	set	to	a	value	that	includes EventRateLimit.

Then,	edit	the	API	server	pod	specification	file	/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml	and	set	the	below	parameters. --enable-admission-plugins=...,EventRateLimit,... --admission-control-config-file=<path/to/configuration/file> Default	Value: 72	|	P a g e By	default,	EventRateLimit	is	not	set. title: Ensure that the admission control plugin AlwaysAdmit is not set
description: Do	not	allow	all	requests.
rationale: Setting	admission	control	plugin	AlwaysAdmit	allows	all	requests	and	do	not	filter	any requests. The	AlwaysAdmit	admission	controller	was	deprecated	in	Kubernetes	v1.13.	Its	behavior was	equivalent	to	turning	off	all	admission	controllers. Impact: Only	requests	explicitly	allowed	by	the	admissions	control	plugins	would	be	served.
audit: Run	the	following	command	on	the	master	node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify	that	if	the	--enable-admission-plugins	argument	is	set,	its	value	does	not	include AlwaysAdmit.
remediation: Edit	the	API	server	pod	specification	file	/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml	on	the	master	node	and	either	remove	the	--enable-admission-plugins parameter,	or	set	it	to	a	value	that	does	not	include	AlwaysAdmit. Default	Value: AlwaysAdmit	is	not	in	the	list	of	default	admission	plugins. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 74	|	P a g e 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/admission-controllers/#alwaysadmit Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 14	Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know 75	|	P a g e
impact: Only	requests	explicitly	allowed	by	the	admissions	control	plugins	would	be	served.
references: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 74	|	P a g e 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/admission-controllers/#alwaysadmit Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 14	Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know 75	|	P a g e
justification: This control requires verification of the kube-apiserver’s admission plugin configuration, specifically ensuring that `AlwaysAdmit` is not enabled. Alibaba Cloud ACK (managed Kubernetes) does not expose the control plane node or its configuration via APIs, CLI, or the console. There is no Alibaba Cloud API, CLI, or managed service that allows inspection or modification of the `kube-apiserver.yaml` or its admission plugins in managed clusters. Therefore, automation is not feasible; manual inspection is required where node access is possible.
manual_summary: 1. Access the Kubernetes master node (control plane) directly via SSH (only possible in self-managed or hybrid clusters, not in fully managed ACK). 2. Run the following command to check the running kube-apiserver process: ```bash ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver ``` 3. Review the command output for the `--enable-admission-plugins` flag. 4. Ensure that the value of `--enable-admission-plugins` does NOT include `AlwaysAdmit`. 5. If `AlwaysAdmit` is present, edit the `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` file to remove it from the list. 6. Save the file and allow the kubelet to automatically restart the API server with the updated configuration. 7. Re-run the check to confirm that `AlwaysAdmit` is no longer present. 8. Document the findings and remediation (if any).

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the admission control plugin AlwaysPullImages is set
description: Always	pull	images.
rationale: Setting	admission	control	policy	to	AlwaysPullImages	forces	every	new	pod	to	pull	the required	images	every	time.	In	a	multi-tenant	cluster	users	can	be	assured	that	their private	images	can	only	be	used	by	those	who	have	the	credentials	to	pull	them.	Without this	admission	control	policy,	once	an	image	has	been	pulled	to	a	node,	any	pod	from	any user	can	use	it	simply	by	knowing	the	image’s	name,	without	any	authorization	check against	the	image	ownership.	When	this	plug-in	is	enabled,	images	are	always	pulled	prior to	starting	containers,	which	means	valid	credentials	are	required. Impact: Credentials	would	be	required	to	pull	the	private	images	every	time.	Also,	in	trusted environments,	this	might	increases	load	on	network,	registry,	and	decreases	speed. This	setting	could	impact	offline	or	isolated	clusters,	which	have	images	pre-loaded	and	do not	have	access	to	a	registry	to	pull	in-use	images.	This	setting	is	not	appropriate	for clusters	which	use	this	configuration.
audit: Run	the	following	command	on	the	master	node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify	that	the	--enable-admission-plugins	argument	is	set	to	a	value	that	includes AlwaysPullImages.
remediation: Edit	the	API	server	pod	specification	file	/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml	on	the	master	node	and	set	the	--enable-admission-plugins	parameter to	include	AlwaysPullImages. 76	|	P a g e --enable-admission-plugins=...,AlwaysPullImages,... Default	Value: By	default,	AlwaysPullImages	is	not	set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/admission-controllers/#alwayspullimages Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. This	setting	could	impact	offline	or	isolated	clusters,	which	have	images	pre-loaded	and	do not	have	access	to	a	registry	to	pull	in-use	images.	This	setting	is	not	appropriate	for clusters	which	use	this	configuration.
references: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/admission-controllers/#alwayspullimages Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 14.4	Protect	Information	With	Access	Control	Lists All	information	stored	on	systems	shall	be	protected	with	file	system,	network	share, claims,	application,	or	database	specific	access	control	lists.	These	controls	will	enforce	the principle	that	only	authorized	individuals	should	have	access	to	the	information	based	on their	need	to	access	the	information	as	a	part	of	their	responsibilities. Version	7 14.6	Protect	Information	through	Access	Control	Lists Protect	all	information	stored	on	systems	with	file	system,	network	share,	claims, application,	or	database	specific	access	control	lists.	These	controls	will	enforce	the principle	that	only	authorized	individuals	should	have	access	to	the	information	based	on their	need	to	access	the	information	as	a	part	of	their	responsibilities. 77	|	P a g e
justification: The verification of the `AlwaysPullImages` admission control plugin requires direct inspection of the kube-apiserver process arguments or its manifest file on the Kubernetes master node. Alibaba Cloud's ACK service does not expose the enabled admission plugins via any API, CLI, or console interface. Both AI opinions acknowledge the lack of API support for this specific configuration, and the official CIS audit step requires SSH access and manual inspection.
manual_summary: 1. SSH into the master node(s) of your Alibaba Cloud ACK Kubernetes cluster. 2. Run the command: `ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver` to list the running kube-apiserver process and its arguments. 3. Check the output for the `--enable-admission-plugins` argument and verify that `AlwaysPullImages` is included in the list. 4. Alternatively, open and inspect the file `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` and ensure the `--enable-admission-plugins` parameter contains `AlwaysPullImages`. 5. Confirm that `AlwaysPullImages` is present as one of the enabled admission plugins. 6. Document your findings for compliance evidence.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the admission control plugin SecurityContextDeny is set if PodSecurityPolicy is not used
description: The	SecurityContextDeny	admission	controller	can	be	used	to	deny	pods	which	make	use	of some	SecurityContext	fields	which	could	allow	for	privilege	escalation	in	the	cluster.	This should	be	used	where	PodSecurityPolicy	is	not	in	place	within	the	cluster.
rationale: SecurityContextDeny	can	be	used	to	provide	a	layer	of	security	for	clusters	which	do	not have	PodSecurityPolicies	enabled. Impact: This	admission	controller	should	only	be	used	where	Pod	Security	Policies	cannot	be	used on	the	cluster,	as	it	can	interact	poorly	with	certain	Pod	Security	Policies
audit: Run	the	following	command	on	the	master	node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify	that	the	--enable-admission-plugins	argument	is	set	to	a	value	that	includes SecurityContextDeny,	if	PodSecurityPolicy	is	not	included.
remediation: Edit	the	API	server	pod	specification	file	/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml	on	the	master	node	and	set	the	--enable-admission-plugins	parameter to	include	SecurityContextDeny,	unless	PodSecurityPolicy	is	already	in	place. --enable-admission-plugins=...,SecurityContextDeny,... Implement	focused	auditing	on	the	use	of	administrative	privileged	functions	and monitor	for	anomalous	behavior. 79	|	P a g e
justification: Both AI opinions correctly identify that Alibaba Cloud ACK does not expose the kube-apiserver’s admission plugin configuration via any Alibaba Cloud API, CLI, or SDK. The only reliable method to verify if the SecurityContextDeny admission plugin is enabled (when PodSecurityPolicy is not used) is by directly inspecting the kube-apiserver process arguments or its manifest file on the master node. Kubernetes-native tools (e.g., kubectl) do not provide visibility into the internal admission plugin configuration in managed ACK clusters, and Alibaba Cloud does not offer a managed API or Config rule for this setting.
manual_summary: 1. **Access the Kubernetes Master Node:** - Obtain SSH access to the master node of your ACK cluster. This typically requires elevated permissions and may not be possible in all managed environments. 2. **Inspect the kube-apiserver Process:** - Run the following command to list the kube-apiserver process and its arguments: ```bash ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver ``` - Alternatively, review the kube-apiserver manifest file (commonly located at `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml`). 3. **Verify Admission Plugins:** - Check the value of the `--enable-admission-plugins` argument. - Confirm that `SecurityContextDeny` is present in the list if `PodSecurityPolicy` is not included. 4. **Expected Secure Configuration:** - The `--enable-admission-plugins` parameter must include `SecurityContextDeny` unless `PodSecurityPolicy` is already enabled. 5. **Confirm Compliance:** - Document the presence of `SecurityContextDeny` in the configuration. If not present and PodSecurityPolicy is also absent, this is non-compliant.

title: Ensure that the admission control plugin NamespaceLifecycle is set

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the admission control plugin PodSecurityPolicy is set

rationale: A	Pod	Security	Policy	is	a	cluster-level	resource	that	controls	the	actions	that	a	pod	can perform	and	what	it	has	the	ability	to	access.	The	PodSecurityPolicy	objects	define	a	set	of conditions	that	a	pod	must	run	with	in	order	to	be	accepted	into	the	system.	Pod	Security Policies	are	comprised	of	settings	and	strategies	that	control	the	security	features	a	pod	has access	to	and	hence	this	must	be	used	to	control	pod	access	permissions. Note: When	the	PodSecurityPolicy	admission	plugin	is	in	use,	there	needs	to	be	at	least	one PodSecurityPolicy	in	place	for	ANY	pods	to	be	admitted. audit: Run	the	following	command	on	the	master	node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify	that	the	--enable-admission-plugins	argument	is	set	to	a	value	that	includes PodSecurityPolicy.

impact: The	policy	objects	must	be	created	and	granted	before	pod	creation	would	be	allowed.

Open `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` and confirm the `--enable-admission-plugins` parameter includes `PodSecurityPolicy`. 4. If changes are made, restart the kube-apiserver for the configuration to take effect. 5. Compliance is confirmed if `PodSecurityPolicy` is present in the enabled admission plugins.

title: Ensure that the --insecure-bind-address argument is not set
description: Do	not	bind	the	insecure	API	service.
rationale: If	you	bind	the	apiserver	to	an	insecure	address,	basically	anyone	who	could	connect	to	it over	the	insecure	port,	would	have	unauthenticated	and	unencrypted	access	to	your master	node.	The	apiserver	doesn't	do	any	authentication	checking	for	insecure	binds	and traffic	to	the	Insecure	API	port	is	not	encrpyted,	allowing	attackers	to	potentially	read sensitive	data	in	transit. Impact: Connections	to	the	API	server	will	require	valid	authentication	credentials.
audit: Run	the	following	command	on	the	master	node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify	that	the	--insecure-bind-address	argument	does	not	exist.
remediation: Edit	the	API	server	pod	specification	file	/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml	on	the	master	node	and	remove	the	--insecure-bind-address parameter. Default	Value: By	default,	the	insecure	bind	address	is	not	set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 88	|	P a g e Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 9.1	Limit	Open	Ports,	Protocols,	and	Services Ensure	that	only	ports,	protocols,	and	services	with	validated	business	needs	are	running on	each	system. Version	7 9.2	Ensure	Only	Approved	Ports,	Protocols	and	Services	Are	Running Ensure	that	only	network	ports,	protocols,	and	services	listening	on	a	system	with validated	business	needs,	are	running	on	each	system. 89	|	P a g e
impact: Connections	to	the	API	server	will	require	valid	authentication	credentials.
references: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 88	|	P a g e Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 9.1	Limit	Open	Ports,	Protocols,	and	Services Ensure	that	only	ports,	protocols,	and	services	with	validated	business	needs	are	running on	each	system. Version	7 9.2	Ensure	Only	Approved	Ports,	Protocols	and	Services	Are	Running Ensure	that	only	network	ports,	protocols,	and	services	listening	on	a	system	with validated	business	needs,	are	running	on	each	system. 89	|	P a g e
justification: The control requires verification that the `--insecure-bind-address` argument is not set for the kube-apiserver process. In Alibaba Cloud ACK managed clusters, direct access to the underlying master node or its process arguments is not exposed via Alibaba Cloud APIs, CLI, or Config. The CIS benchmark itself explicitly states this control cannot be audited or modified in ACK managed clusters, confirming that no programmatic or API-based method exists for this check. Therefore, only manual inspection is possible in self-managed Kubernetes clusters where SSH access to the master node is available.
manual_summary: 1. **Access the Master Node:** SSH into the master node of your Kubernetes cluster using appropriate credentials. 2. **List kube-apiserver Processes:** Run the command: `ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver` 3. **Verify Process Arguments:** Examine the output for any occurrence of the `--insecure-bind-address` argument. 4. **Expected Secure Configuration:** Confirm that the `--insecure-bind-address` argument is completely absent from the kube-apiserver process arguments. 5. **Document Compliance:** Record your findings, noting the absence (compliance) or presence (non-compliance) of the argument. 6. **Why Automation Isn't Feasible:** Alibaba Cloud ACK managed clusters do not expose master node process arguments via API, CLI, or Config, making automation impossible for this control.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the --secure-port argument is not set to 0
description: Do	not	disable	the	secure	port.
rationale: The	secure	port	is	used	to	serve	https	with	authentication	and	authorization.	If	you	disable it,	no	https	traffic	is	served	and	all	traffic	is	served	unencrypted. Impact: You	need	to	set	the	API	Server	up	with	the	right	TLS	certificates.
audit: Run	the	following	command	on	the	master	node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify	that	the	--secure-port	argument	is	either	not	set	or	is	set	to	an	integer	value between	1	and	65535.
remediation: Edit	the	API	server	pod	specification	file	/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml	on	the	master	node	and	either	remove	the	--secure-port	parameter	or set	it	to	a	different	(non-zero)	desired	port. Default	Value: By	default,	port	6443	is	used	as	the	secure	port. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ Additional	Information: 92	|	P a g e This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 14.2	Encrypt	All	Sensitive	Information	Over	Less-trusted	Networks All	communication	of	sensitive	information	over	less-trusted	networks	should	be encrypted.	Whenever	information	flows	over	a	network	with	a	lower	trust	level,	the information	should	be	encrypted. Version	7 14.4	Encrypt	All	Sensitive	Information	in	Transit Encrypt	all	sensitive	information	in	transit. 93	|	P a g e
impact: You	need	to	set	the	API	Server	up	with	the	right	TLS	certificates.
references: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ Additional	Information: 92	|	P a g e This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 14.2	Encrypt	All	Sensitive	Information	Over	Less-trusted	Networks All	communication	of	sensitive	information	over	less-trusted	networks	should	be encrypted.	Whenever	information	flows	over	a	network	with	a	lower	trust	level,	the information	should	be	encrypted. Version	7 14.4	Encrypt	All	Sensitive	Information	in	Transit Encrypt	all	sensitive	information	in	transit. 93	|	P a g e
justification: The control requires direct inspection of the Kubernetes API server process arguments or its manifest file on the master node to verify the `--secure-port` setting. Alibaba Cloud does not expose this configuration via APIs, CLI, or managed service interfaces for ACK clusters, and the CIS benchmark itself notes this cannot be audited or modified in managed clusters. Both AI opinions acknowledge the lack of automation capability, and no Alibaba Cloud service (including Config or CloudMonitor) can currently access or validate this specific runtime or manifest parameter.
manual_summary: 1. **Access the Master Node:** SSH into the master node of your Kubernetes cluster where the kube-apiserver is running. 2. **Check Running Process Arguments:** Run the following command to inspect the kube-apiserver process: `ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver` Look for the `--secure-port` argument in the output. 3. **(Optional) Inspect Manifest File:** Alternatively, open the API server manifest file: `cat /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` Search for the `--secure-port` parameter in the command section. 4. **Verify Compliance:** - Ensure the `--secure-port` argument is either not set (defaulting to 6443) or set to a value between 1 and 65535 (not 0). - If `--secure-port=0` is found, this is non-compliant. 5. **Document Findings:** Record the configuration and compliance status for audit purposes.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the --profiling argument is set to false
description: Disable	profiling,	if	not	needed.
rationale: Profiling	allows	for	the	identification	of	specific	performance	bottlenecks.	It	generates	a significant	amount	of	program	data	that	could	potentially	be	exploited	to	uncover	system and	program	details.	If	you	are	not	experiencing	any	bottlenecks	and	do	not	need	the profiler	for	troubleshooting	purposes,	it	is	recommended	to	turn	it	off	to	reduce	the potential	attack	surface. Impact: Profiling	information	would	not	be	available.
audit: Run	the	following	command	on	the	master	node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify	that	the	--profiling	argument	is	set	to	false.
remediation: Edit	the	API	server	pod	specification	file	/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml	on	the	master	node	and	set	the	below	parameter. --profiling=false Default	Value: By	default,	profiling	is	enabled. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/devel/profi ling.md 94	|	P a g e Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 14	Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know 95	|	P a g e

CIS	Controls: Version	6 14	Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know 95	|	P a g e
justification: The control requires verification of the `--profiling` argument in the kube-apiserver process, which is only accessible by directly inspecting the process arguments or configuration files on the master node. Alibaba Cloud APIs, CLI, Config, or managed ACK services do not expose this parameter for audit or modification, as explicitly stated in the control and confirmed by both AI opinions. While log or monitoring services could theoretically capture some process information, they do not provide reliable or supported automation for this specific check in ACK managed clusters.
manual_summary: 1. **Access the master node:** SSH into the Kubernetes master node where the kube-apiserver is running. 2. **Check the kube-apiserver process:** Run `ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver` to list the running kube-apiserver process and its arguments. 3. **Verify the `--profiling` argument:** Inspect the output for the `--profiling` flag. Confirm it is set to `--profiling=false`. If the flag is missing or set to `true`, profiling is enabled (default). 4. **Confirm compliance:** Compliance is achieved only if `--profiling=false` is explicitly set in the process arguments. 5. **(If remediation is needed):** Edit `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` to set `--profiling=false`, then restart the kube-apiserver.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the --request-timeout argument is set as appropriate
description: Set	global	request	timeout	for	API	server	requests	as	appropriate.
rationale: Setting	global	request	timeout	allows	extending	the	API	server	request	timeout	limit	to	a duration	appropriate	to	the	user's	connection	speed.	By	default,	it	is	set	to	60	seconds which	might	be	problematic	on	slower	connections	making	cluster	resources	inaccessible once	the	data	volume	for	requests	exceeds	what	can	be	transmitted	in	60	seconds.	But, setting	this	timeout	limit	to	be	too	large	can	exhaust	the	API	server	resources	making	it prone	to	Denial-of-Service	attack.	Hence,	it	is	recommended	to	set	this	limit	as	appropriate and	change	the	default	limit	of	60	seconds	only	if	needed. Impact: None
audit: Run	the	following	command	on	the	master	node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify	that	the	--request-timeout	argument	is	either	not	set	or	set	to	an	appropriate value.
remediation: Edit	the	API	server	pod	specification	file	/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml	and	set	the	below	parameter	as	appropriate	and	if	needed.	For	example, --request-timeout=300s Default	Value: By	default,	--request-timeout	is	set	to	60	seconds. 104	|	P a g e References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/51415 Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 14.6	Enforce	Detailed	Audit	Logging	For	Sensitive	Information Enforce	detailed	audit	logging	for	access	to	nonpublic	data	and	special	authentication	for sensitive	data. CIS	Controls: Version	6 14.6	Enforce	Detailed	Audit	Logging	For	Sensitive	Information Enforce	detailed	audit	logging	for	access	to	nonpublic	data	and	special	authentication	for sensitive	data. Version	7 14.9	Enforce	Detail	Logging	for	Access	or	Changes	to	Sensitive	Data Enforce	detailed	audit	logging	for	access	to	sensitive	data	or	changes	to	sensitive	data (utilizing	tools	such	as	File	Integrity	Monitoring	or	Security	Information	and	Event Monitoring). 105	|	P a g e

The audit and remediation steps require direct access to the master node and the underlying manifest files, which is not possible in Alibaba Cloud's managed ACK clusters. manual_summary: 1. **Access the Master Node:** - Obtain SSH access to the master node of the Kubernetes cluster (only possible in self-managed clusters, not ACK managed clusters). 2. **Check kube-apiserver Process Arguments:** - Run the command: `ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver` - Review the output for the presence and value of the `--request-timeout` argument. 3. **Verify Configuration:** - Confirm that `--request-timeout` is either not set (defaults to 60s) or is set to an appropriate value based on your organization's risk tolerance and network conditions (e.g., not excessively high to avoid DoS risk). 4. **Remediate if Needed:** - If adjustment is required, edit `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` and set the `--request-timeout` parameter as appropriate. - Restart the kube-apiserver if necessary for the change to take effect. 5. **Document the Check:** - Record the observed value and any changes made for audit purposes.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the --service-account-lookup argument is set to true
description: Validate	service	account	before	validating	token.
rationale: If	--service-account-lookup	is	not	enabled,	the	apiserver	only	verifies	that	the authentication	token	is	valid,	and	does	not	validate	that	the	service	account	token mentioned	in	the	request	is	actually	present	in	etcd.	This	allows	using	a	service	account token	even	after	the	corresponding	service	account	is	deleted.	This	is	an	example	of	time	of check	to	time	of	use	security	issue. Impact: None
audit: Run	the	following	command	on	the	master	node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify	that	if	the	--service-account-lookup	argument	exists	it	is	set	to	true.
remediation: Edit	the	API	server	pod	specification	file	/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml	on	the	master	node	and	set	the	below	parameter. --service-account-lookup=true Alternatively,	you	can	delete	the	--service-account-lookup	parameter	from	this	file	so that	the	default	takes	effect. Default	Value: By	default,	--service-account-lookup	argument	is	set	to	true. 106	|	P a g e References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/24167 3. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Time_of_check_to_time_of_use Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 16	Account	Monitoring	and	Control Account	Monitoring	and	Control 107	|	P a g e
references: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/24167 3. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Time_of_check_to_time_of_use Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 16	Account	Monitoring	and	Control Account	Monitoring	and	Control 107	|	P a g e
justification: The audit requires direct inspection of the kube-apiserver process arguments or the `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` file on the master node to verify the `--service-account-lookup` parameter. Alibaba Cloud’s managed ACK clusters do not expose master node access or this configuration via any public API, CLI, or Alibaba Cloud Config rule. No Alibaba Cloud service currently provides programmatic access to this specific kube-apiserver argument, making automation infeasible.
manual_summary: 1. **Access the Master Node:** - SSH into the Kubernetes master node (only possible for self-managed clusters, not ACK managed clusters). 2. **Inspect the kube-apiserver Arguments:** - Run: `ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver` - Look for the `--service-account-lookup` argument in the process list. 3. **Check the Configuration File:** - Open `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` with a text editor. - Search for the `--service-account-lookup` parameter and confirm it is set to `true` or omitted (default is `true`). 4. **Verify Compliance:** - Compliance is achieved if `--service-account-lookup=true` is present or the parameter is omitted (defaulting to `true`). - Non-compliance if the parameter is set to `false`. 5. **Document Findings:** - Record the configuration and process output as evidence.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the --service-account-key-file argument is set as appropriate
description: Explicitly	set	a	service	account	public	key	file	for	service	accounts	on	the	apiserver.
rationale: By	default,	if	no	--service-account-key-file	is	specified	to	the	apiserver,	it	uses	the private	key	from	the	TLS	serving	certificate	to	verify	service	account	tokens.	To	ensure	that the	keys	for	service	account	tokens	could	be	rotated	as	needed,	a	separate	public/private key	pair	should	be	used	for	signing	service	account	tokens.	Hence,	the	public	key	should	be specified	to	the	apiserver	with	--service-account-key-file. Impact: The	corresponding	private	key	must	be	provided	to	the	controller	manager.	You	would need	to	securely	maintain	the	key	file	and	rotate	the	keys	based	on	your	organization's	key rotation	policy.
audit: Run	the	following	command	on	the	master	node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify	that	the	--service-account-key-file	argument	exists	and	is	set	as	appropriate.
remediation: Edit	the	API	server	pod	specification	file	/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml	on	the	master	node	and	set	the	--service-account-key-file	parameter to	the	public	key	file	for	service	accounts: --service-account-key-file=<filename> Default	Value: By	default,	--service-account-key-file	argument	is	not	set. 108	|	P a g e References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/24167 Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 3	Secure	Configurations	for	Hardware	and	Software	on	Mobile	Devices,	Laptops, Workstations,	and	Servers Secure	Configurations	for	Hardware	and	Software	on	Mobile	Devices,	Laptops, Workstations,	and	Servers 109	|	P a g e
impact: The	corresponding	private	key	must	be	provided	to	the	controller	manager.	You	would need	to	securely	maintain	the	key	file	and	rotate	the	keys	based	on	your	organization's	key rotation	policy.
references: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/24167 Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 3	Secure	Configurations	for	Hardware	and	Software	on	Mobile	Devices,	Laptops, Workstations,	and	Servers Secure	Configurations	for	Hardware	and	Software	on	Mobile	Devices,	Laptops, Workstations,	and	Servers 109	|	P a g e
justification: This control requires verification of the `--service-account-key-file` argument in the kube-apiserver process, which is only accessible via direct inspection of the master node’s configuration or running processes. Alibaba Cloud’s managed Kubernetes (ACK) does not expose the control plane or its configuration through any API, CLI, or managed service interface, making automation impossible for both managed and most self-managed scenarios. Both AI opinions converge on the necessity of manual inspection, and this is confirmed by the control’s own documentation.
manual_summary: 1. **Access the Master Node:** - SSH into the master node of your Kubernetes cluster (only possible for self-managed clusters on ECS; not possible for ACK managed clusters). 2. **Check the kube-apiserver Process:** - Run the following command to list the kube-apiserver process and its arguments: ```bash ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver ``` 3. **Verify the Argument:** - Inspect the output and confirm that the `--service-account-key-file` argument is present and set to the correct public key file for service accounts. 4. **Expected Secure Configuration:** - The `--service-account-key-file` argument should be explicitly set to a separate public key file, not left unset or using the default TLS serving certificate. 5. **Confirm Compliance:** - Compliance is achieved if the argument is present and correctly configured as described above.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the --etcd-certfile and --etcd-keyfile arguments are set as appropriate
description: etcd	should	be	configured	to	make	use	of	TLS	encryption	for	client	connections.
rationale: etcd	is	a	highly-available	key	value	store	used	by	Kubernetes	deployments	for	persistent storage	of	all	of	its	REST	API	objects.	These	objects	are	sensitive	in	nature	and	should	be protected	by	client	authentication.	This	requires	the	API	server	to	identify	itself	to	the	etcd server	using	a	client	certificate	and	key. Impact: TLS	and	client	certificate	authentication	must	be	configured	for	etcd.
audit: Run	the	following	command	on	the	master	node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify	that	the	--etcd-certfile	and	--etcd-keyfile	arguments	exist	and	they	are	set	as appropriate.
remediation: Follow	the	Kubernetes	documentation	and	set	up	the	TLS	connection	between	the apiserver	and	etcd.	Then,	edit	the	API	server	pod	specification	file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml	on	the	master	node	and	set	the	etcd certificate	and	key	file	parameters. --etcd-certfile=<path/to/client-certificate-file> --etcd-keyfile=<path/to/client-key-file> Default	Value: By	default,	--etcd-certfile	and	--etcd-keyfile	arguments	are	not	set 110	|	P a g e References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. https://coreos.com/etcd/docs/latest/op-guide/security.html Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 9	Limitation	and	Control	of	Network	Ports,	Protocols,	and	Services Limitation	and	Control	of	Network	Ports,	Protocols,	and	Services 111	|	P a g e
impact: TLS	and	client	certificate	authentication	must	be	configured	for	etcd.
references: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. https://coreos.com/etcd/docs/latest/op-guide/security.html Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 9	Limitation	and	Control	of	Network	Ports,	Protocols,	and	Services Limitation	and	Control	of	Network	Ports,	Protocols,	and	Services 111	|	P a g e
justification: The audit requires direct inspection of the kube-apiserver process arguments on the master node to verify the presence and correctness of the `--etcd-certfile` and `--etcd-keyfile` flags. Alibaba Cloud APIs, ACK console, or CLI tools do not expose process-level arguments or the contents of static pod manifest files on managed nodes. Therefore, this check cannot be automated through Alibaba Cloud’s native interfaces and requires SSH access to the master node.
manual_summary: 1. **Access the Master Node:** SSH into the Kubernetes master node where the kube-apiserver is running. Obtain credentials or keys as per your organization’s access policy. 2. **Inspect the kube-apiserver Process:** Run the following command to locate the kube-apiserver process and view its arguments: ```bash ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver ``` 3. **Verify Arguments:** In the process output, confirm that both `--etcd-certfile=<path/to/client-certificate-file>` and `--etcd-keyfile=<path/to/client-key-file>` are present and point to valid certificate and key files. 4. **Check File Validity:** Optionally, verify that the referenced files exist and have appropriate permissions. 5. **Expected Secure Configuration:** Both arguments must be present and reference valid, non-default certificate and key files to ensure TLS encryption for etcd client connections. 6. **Confirm Compliance:** Document the presence and correctness of these arguments. If either is missing or set incorrectly, the control is not compliant.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the API Server only makes use of Strong Cryptographic Ciphers
description: Ensure	that	the	API	server	is	configured	to	only	use	strong	cryptographic	ciphers.
rationale: TLS	ciphers	have	had	a	number	of	known	vulnerabilities	and	weaknesses,	which	can reduce	the	protection	provided	by	them.	By	default	Kubernetes	supports	a	number	of	TLS ciphersuites	including	some	that	have	security	concerns,	weakening	the	protection provided. Impact: API	server	clients	that	cannot	support	modern	cryptographic	ciphers	will	not	be	able	to make	connections	to	the	API	server.
audit: Run	the	following	command	on	the	master	node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify	that	the	--tls-cipher-suites	argument	is	set	as	outlined	in	the	remediation procedure	below.
remediation: Edit	the	API	server	pod	specification	file	/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml on	the	master	node	and	set	the	below	parameter. --tls-cipher- suites=TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256,TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM _SHA256,TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_CHACHA20_POLY1305,TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM _SHA384,TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_CHACHA20_POLY1305,TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM _SHA384 Default	Value: By	default	the	Kubernetes	API	server	supports	a	wide	range	of	TLS	ciphers 122	|	P a g e References: 1. https://github.com/ssllabs/research/wiki/SSL-and-TLS-Deployment-Best- Practices#23-use-secure-cipher-suites Additional	Information: The	list	chosen	above	should	be	fine	for	modern	clients.	It's	essentially	the	list	from	the Mozilla	"Modern	cipher"	option	with	the	ciphersuites	supporting	CBC	mode	removed,	as CBC	has	traditionally	had	a	lot	of	issues This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 3.4	Use	Only	Secure	Channels	For	Remote	System	Administration Perform	all	remote	administration	of	servers,	workstation,	network	devices,	and	similar equipment	over	secure	channels.	Protocols	such	as	telnet,	VNC,	RDP,	or	others	that	do	not actively	support	strong	encryption	should	only	be	used	if	they	are	performed	over	a secondary	encryption	channel,	such	as	SSL,	TLS	or	IPSEC. Version	7 4.5	Use	Multifactor	Authentication	For	All	Administrative	Access Use	multi-factor	authentication	and	encrypted	channels	for	all	administrative	account access. 123	|	P a g e 1.3 Controller Manager This	section	contains	recommendations	relating	to	Controller	Manager	configuration	flags
impact: API	server	clients	that	cannot	support	modern	cryptographic	ciphers	will	not	be	able	to make	connections	to	the	API	server.
references: 1. https://github.com/ssllabs/research/wiki/SSL-and-TLS-Deployment-Best- Practices#23-use-secure-cipher-suites Additional	Information: The	list	chosen	above	should	be	fine	for	modern	clients.	It's	essentially	the	list	from	the Mozilla	"Modern	cipher"	option	with	the	ciphersuites	supporting	CBC	mode	removed,	as CBC	has	traditionally	had	a	lot	of	issues This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. Protocols	such	as	telnet,	VNC,	RDP,	or	others	that	do	not actively	support	strong	encryption	should	only	be	used	if	they	are	performed	over	a secondary	encryption	channel,	such	as	SSL,	TLS	or	IPSEC. Version	7 4.5	Use	Multifactor	Authentication	For	All	Administrative	Access Use	multi-factor	authentication	and	encrypted	channels	for	all	administrative	account access. 123	|	P a g e 1.3 Controller Manager This	section	contains	recommendations	relating	to	Controller	Manager	configuration	flags
justification: Both AI opinions correctly identify that verifying the Kubernetes API server’s TLS cipher suite configuration requires direct inspection of the server’s process arguments or configuration files. Alibaba Cloud does not expose the `--tls-cipher-suites` parameter for the API server via any public API, CLI, or managed service interface (including ACK). This check fundamentally requires SSH access to the master node or equivalent privileged access to the pod manifest, which cannot be achieved through Alibaba Cloud APIs or automation tools.
manual_summary: 1. **SSH into the Kubernetes Master Node:** - Use your preferred SSH client to access the master node of your Kubernetes cluster. 2. **Check the API Server Process Arguments:** - Run the following command to locate the kube-apiserver process and its arguments: ``` ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver ``` - Look for the `--tls-cipher-suites` argument in the process output. 3. **Verify Cipher Suites:** - Ensure that the `--tls-cipher-suites` argument is present and only includes the following strong ciphers: ``` TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256, TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256, TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_CHACHA20_POLY1305, TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384, TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_CHACHA20_POLY1305, TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384 ``` - If the argument is missing or includes weaker ciphers, this is non-compliant. 4. **Confirm Compliance:** - Document the presence and exact value of the `--tls-cipher-suites` argument. - If remediation is needed, manually edit `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` to set the correct cipher suites, then restart the API server.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the --terminated-pod-gc-threshold argument is set as appropriate
description: Activate	garbage	collector	on	pod	termination,	as	appropriate.
rationale: Garbage	collection	is	important	to	ensure	sufficient	resource	availability	and	avoiding degraded	performance	and	availability.	In	the	worst	case,	the	system	might	crash	or	just	be unusable	for	a	long	period	of	time.	The	current	setting	for	garbage	collection	is	12,500 terminated	pods	which	might	be	too	high	for	your	system	to	sustain. Direct inspection of this setting requires access to the master node’s file system or process list, which is only possible in self-managed Kubernetes clusters on ECS. No Alibaba Cloud native service or API currently provides visibility or control over this parameter in managed environments, making automation infeasible.
manual_summary: 1. **Access the Master Node:** - SSH into the Kubernetes master node where the kube-controller-manager is running (only possible in self-managed clusters). 2. **Check the Running Configuration:** - Execute: `ps -ef | grep kube-controller-manager` - Locate the `--terminated-pod-gc-threshold` argument in the process output. 3. **Verify the Configuration:** - Ensure the value for `--terminated-pod-gc-threshold` is set to an appropriate threshold for your environment (default is 12500; recommended value may be lower depending on system resources). 4. **Modify if Necessary:** - If adjustment is needed, edit `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-controller-manager.yaml` and set the `--terminated-pod-gc-threshold` argument to the desired value. 5. **Confirm Compliance:** - Restart the kube-controller-manager if required and re-run the check to confirm the new setting is active.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the --profiling argument is set to false
description: Disable	profiling,	if	not	needed.
rationale: Profiling	allows	for	the	identification	of	specific	performance	bottlenecks.	It	generates	a significant	amount	of	program	data	that	could	potentially	be	exploited	to	uncover	system and	program	details.	If	you	are	not	experiencing	any	bottlenecks	and	do	not	need	the profiler	for	troubleshooting	purposes,	it	is	recommended	to	turn	it	off	to	reduce	the potential	attack	surface. title: Ensure that the --use-service-account-credentials argument is set to true
description: Use	individual	service	account	credentials	for	each	controller.
rationale: The	controller	manager	creates	a	service	account	per	controller	in	the	kube-system namespace,	generates	a	credential	for	it,	and	builds	a	dedicated	API	client	with	that	service account	credential	for	each	controller	loop	to	use.	Setting	the	--use-service-account- credentials	to	true	runs	each	control	loop	within	the	controller	manager	using	a	separate service	account	credential.	When	used	in	combination	with	RBAC,	this	ensures	that	the control	loops	run	with	the	minimum	permissions	required	to	perform	their	intended	tasks. Impact: Whatever	authorizer	is	configured	for	the	cluster,	it	must	grant	sufficient	permissions	to the	service	accounts	to	perform	their	intended	tasks.	When	using	the	RBAC	authorizer, those	roles	are	created	and	bound	to	the	appropriate	service	accounts	in	the	kube-system namespace	automatically	with	default	roles	and	rolebindings	that	are	auto-reconciled	on startup. If	using	other	authorization	methods	(ABAC,	Webhook,	etc),	the	cluster	deployer	is responsible	for	granting	appropriate	permissions	to	the	service	accounts	(the	required permissions	can	be	seen	by	inspecting	the	controller-roles.yaml	and	controller-role- bindings.yaml	files	for	the	RBAC	roles.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the --root-ca-file argument is set as appropriate
description: Allow	pods	to	verify	the	API	server's	serving	certificate	before	establishing	connections.
rationale: Processes	running	within	pods	that	need	to	contact	the	API	server	must	verify	the	API server's	serving	certificate.	Failing	to	do	so	could	be	a	subject	to	man-in-the-middle	attacks. Providing	the	root	certificate	for	the	API	server's	serving	certificate	to	the	controller manager	with	the	--root-ca-file	argument	allows	the	controller	manager	to	inject	the trusted	bundle	into	pods	so	that	they	can	verify	TLS	connections	to	the	API	server. Impact: You	need	to	setup	and	maintain	root	certificate	authority	file.
audit: Run	the	following	command	on	the	master	node: ps -ef | grep kube-controller-manager Verify	that	the	--root-ca-file	argument	exists	and	is	set	to	a	certificate	bundle	file containing	the	root	certificate	for	the	API	server's	serving	certificate.

Version	7 14.4	Encrypt	All	Sensitive	Information	in	Transit Encrypt	all	sensitive	information	in	transit. 133	|	P a g e
impact: You	need	to	setup	and	maintain	root	certificate	authority	file.

manual_summary: 1. **Access the Master Node:** - For self-managed Kubernetes clusters, SSH into the master node using appropriate credentials. - For managed ACK clusters, note that direct access is not possible; contact Alibaba Cloud support if required. 2. **Inspect the Controller Manager Process:** - Run the following command to locate the kube-controller-manager process: `ps -ef | grep kube-controller-manager` 3. **Check for --root-ca-file Argument:** - Review the process arguments to verify that `--root-ca-file` is present and points to a valid certificate bundle file containing the root certificate for the API server. 4. **(If Using Static Pods) Inspect the Pod Manifest:** - Open `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-controller-manager.yaml` and confirm that the `--root-ca-file` parameter is set to the correct file path. 5. **Expected Secure Configuration:** - The `--root-ca-file` argument must be set and reference a valid certificate bundle file. 6. **Confirm Compliance:** - Compliance is achieved if the argument exists and the referenced file contains the correct root CA certificate for the API server.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the --client-cert-auth argument is set to true
description: Enable	client	authentication	on	etcd	service.
rationale: etcd	is	a	highly-available	key	value	store	used	by	Kubernetes	deployments	for	persistent storage	of	all	of	its	REST	API	objects. Impact: All	clients	attempting	to	access	the	etcd	server	will	require	a	valid	client	certificate.

justification: The audit requires verification of the etcd process arguments, specifically `--client-cert-auth`, which are set at the process or pod specification level on the master node. Alibaba Cloud APIs, CLI, or ACK console do not expose etcd process arguments or manifest file contents for managed clusters. While Kubernetes-native tools like `kubectl` can sometimes inspect pod specs, in ACK the etcd component is managed by the control plane and is not accessible via standard Kubernetes API or Alibaba Cloud APIs. Therefore, SSH access and direct inspection are required.
manual_summary: 1. SSH into the master node of your Kubernetes cluster where the etcd service is running. 2. Run the following command to list the running etcd process and its arguments: ```bash ps -ef | grep etcd ``` 3. Examine the output to locate the `--client-cert-auth` argument. 4. Confirm that `--client-cert-auth` is explicitly set to `true`. 5. If the argument is missing or set to `false`, edit the etcd pod specification file (usually `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml`) to include or update `--client-cert-auth="true"`. 6. Save the file and allow the etcd pod to restart with the new configuration. 7. Re-run the `ps -ef | grep etcd` command to confirm the change.
