justification: This control requires inspection of Kubernetes Pod Security Policies (PSPs) to ensure privileged containers are not generally permitted. PSPs are Kubernetes-native resources managed within the Kubernetes cluster and are not exposed or manageable via Alibaba Cloud APIs, CLI, or Config. The audit steps explicitly require the use of `kubectl` commands to query and inspect PSPs, which cannot be performed through Alibaba Cloud-native automation tools. While scripting with `kubectl` is possible, it still requires direct cluster access and is not achievable via Alibaba Cloud's API surface.
manual_summary: 1. **Access the Kubernetes Cluster:** - Ensure you have `kubectl` access to the target Alibaba Cloud ACK cluster. 2. **List Pod Security Policies:** - Run: `kubectl get psp` - This lists all Pod Security Policies defined in the cluster. 3. **Inspect Each PSP for Privileged Flag:** - For each PSP listed, run: `kubectl get psp <name> -o=jsonpath='{.spec.privileged}'` - Check the output for each policy. 4. **Verify Compliance:** - Confirm that at least one PSP exists where `.spec.privileged` is omitted or set to `false`. - If all PSPs return `true`, the cluster is non-compliant. 5. **Document Findings:** - Record the names and privileged status of all PSPs reviewed.

manual_summary: 1. Access the Alibaba Cloud ACK (Kubernetes) cluster with appropriate credentials. 2. Run the following command to list all PodSecurityPolicies (PSPs): `kubectl get psp` 3. Document your findings and ensure that only necessary PSPs allow `hostPID: true`, and that RBAC controls are in place for those exceptions.

manual_summary: 1. **Access the Kubernetes Cluster:** Use the Alibaba Cloud console or your preferred method to access the Kubernetes cluster where PSPs are configured. 2. **List All PodSecurityPolicies:** Run the following command to retrieve all PSPs: ``` kubectl get psp ``` 3. **Check Each PSP for hostIPC:** For each PSP listed, run: ``` kubectl get psp <psp-name> -o=jsonpath='{.spec.hostIPC}' ``` Replace `<psp-name>` with the actual name of each PSP. 4. **Verify Compliance:** Ensure that there is at least one PSP where the output is either `false` or empty (field omitted), confirming that containers are not permitted to share the host IPC namespace by default. 5. **Document Findings:** Record which PSPs, if any, have `hostIPC: true`. source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Minimize the admission of containers with allowPrivilegeEscalation
description: Do	not	generally	permit	containers	to	be	run	with	the	allowPrivilegeEscalation	flag	set to	true.
rationale: A	container	running	with	the	allowPrivilegeEscalation	flag	set	to	true	may	have processes	that	can	gain	more	privileges	than	their	parent. There	should	be	at	least	one	PodSecurityPolicy	(PSP)	defined	which	does	not	permit containers	to	allow	privilege	escalation.	The	option	exists	(and	is	defaulted	to	true)	to permit	setuid	binaries	to	run. If	you	have	need	to	run	containers	which	use	setuid	binaries	or	require	privilege	escalation, this	should	be	defined	in	a	separate	PSP	and	you	should	carefully	check	RBAC	controls	to ensure	that	only	limited	service	accounts	and	users	are	given	permission	to	access	that PSP. manual_summary: 1. **Access the Kubernetes Cluster:** Use `kubectl` with appropriate credentials to connect to your Alibaba Cloud ACK cluster. 2. **List PodSecurityPolicies:** Run: ``` kubectl get psp ``` to list all PodSecurityPolicies in the cluster. 3. **Check allowPrivilegeEscalation Setting:** For each PSP, run: ``` kubectl get psp <psp-name> -o=jsonpath='{.spec.allowPrivilegeEscalation}' ``` Review the output for each PSP. 4. **Verify Compliance:** Ensure that at least one PSP exists where `.spec.allowPrivilegeEscalation` is either omitted or explicitly set to `false`. - If all PSPs have `allowPrivilegeEscalation: true`, the cluster is non-compliant. - If at least one PSP restricts privilege escalation, the cluster is compliant. 5. **Document Findings:** Record which PSP(s) enforce the restriction and retain command outputs for audit evidence.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Minimize the admission of root containers
description: Do	not	generally	permit	containers	to	be	run	as	the	root	user.
rationale: Containers	may	run	as	any	Linux	user.	Containers	which	run	as	the	root	user,	whilst constrained	by	Container	Runtime	security	features	still	have	a	escalated	likelihood	of container	breakout. Ideally,	all	containers	should	run	as	a	defined	non-UID	0	user. There	should	be	at	least	one	PodSecurityPolicy	(PSP)	defined	which	does	not	permit	root users	in	a	container. If	you	need	to	run	root	containers,	this	should	be	defined	in	a	separate	PSP	and	you	should carefully	check	RBAC	controls	to	ensure	that	only	limited	service	accounts	and	users	are given	permission	to	access	that	PSP. Impact: Pods	with	containers	which	run	as	the	root	user	will	not	be	permitted.

Default	Value: By	default,	PodSecurityPolicies	are	not	defined. Implement	focused	auditing	on	the	use	of	administrative	privileged	functions	and monitor	for	anomalous	behavior. Implement	focused	auditing	on	the	use	of	administrative	privileged	functions	and monitor	for	anomalous	behavior. Automation is possible with custom scripts or CI/CD integration, but not through Alibaba Cloud's native compliance or API tooling, which is the scope for this decision.
manual_summary: 1. Obtain `kubectl` access credentials for the target Alibaba Cloud ACK (Kubernetes) cluster. 2. List all PodSecurityPolicies by running: `kubectl get psp` 3. For each PSP, check the `runAsUser.rule` configuration with: `kubectl get psp <name> -o=jsonpath='{.spec.runAsUser.rule}'` 4. Verify that at least one PSP has `runAsUser.rule` set to `MustRunAsNonRoot` or `MustRunAs` with a UID range that does not include 0. 5. Confirm compliance if such a PSP exists and is enforced for relevant workloads.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Minimize the admission of containers with the NET_RAW capability
description: Do	not	generally	permit	containers	with	the	potentially	dangerous	NET_RAW	capability.
rationale: Containers	run	with	a	default	set	of	capabilities	as	assigned	by	the	Container	Runtime.	By default	this	can	include	potentially	dangerous	capabilities.	With	Docker	as	the	container runtime	the	NET_RAW	capability	is	enabled	which	may	be	misused	by	malicious containers. Ideally,	all	containers	should	drop	this	capability. There	should	be	at	least	one	PodSecurityPolicy	(PSP)	defined	which	prevents	containers with	the	NET_RAW	capability	from	launching. If	you	need	to	run	containers	with	this	capability,	this	should	be	defined	in	a	separate	PSP and	you	should	carefully	check	RBAC	controls	to	ensure	that	only	limited	service	accounts and	users	are	given	permission	to	access	that	PSP. Impact: Pods	with	containers	which	run	with	the	NET_RAW	capability	will	not	be	permitted.
audit: Get	the	set	of	PSPs	with	the	following	command: kubectl get psp For	each	PSP,	check	whether	NET_RAW	is	disabled: kubectl get psp <name> -o=jsonpath='{.spec.requiredDropCapabilities}' Verify	that	there	is	at	least	one	PSP	which	returns	NET_RAW	or	ALL.
remediation: 226	|	P a g e Create	a	PSP	as	described	in	the	Kubernetes	documentation,	ensuring	that	the .spec.requiredDropCapabilities	is	set	to	include	either	NET_RAW	or	ALL. Default	Value: By	default,	PodSecurityPolicies	are	not	defined. Implement	focused	auditing	on	the	use	of	administrative	privileged	functions	and monitor	for	anomalous	behavior. Therefore, this control can be reliably and repeatably audited using automation.
automation_summary: - Alibaba Cloud Resource/Service: Alibaba Cloud Container Service for Kubernetes (ACK) – PodSecurityPolicies (PSPs) - API/CLI Method: Use `kubectl get psp` and `kubectl get psp <name> -o=jsonpath='{.spec.requiredDropCapabilities}'` via automation (e.g., shell script, Aliyun CLI, or Kubernetes API client). - Properties to Check: `.spec.requiredDropCapabilities` field of each PSP. - Pass Criteria: At least one PSP exists where `NET_RAW` or `ALL` is present in `.spec.requiredDropCapabilities`. - Fail Criteria: No PSPs have `NET_RAW` or `ALL` in `.spec.requiredDropCapabilities`.

manual_summary: 1. **Access the ACK Cluster:** - Use `kubectl` to authenticate and connect to your Alibaba Cloud ACK Kubernetes cluster. 2. **List All PodSecurityPolicies:** - Run: `kubectl get psp` to see all PSPs configured in the cluster. 3. **Inspect Each PSP for allowedCapabilities:** - For each PSP, run: `kubectl get psp <psp-name> -o yaml` - Examine the `allowedCapabilities` field in the output. 4. **Verify Compliance:** - Ensure that `allowedCapabilities` is either not present or is set to an empty array (`[]`) in all PSPs. - If any PSP has `allowedCapabilities` set to anything other than an empty array, the cluster is non-compliant. 5. **Document Findings:** - Record which PSPs were inspected and their `allowedCapabilities` settings for audit evidence.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Minimize the admission of containers with capabilities assigned
description: There	are	a	variety	of	CNI	plugins	available	for	Kubernetes.	If	the	CNI	in	use	does	not support	Network	Policies	it	may	not	be	possible	to	effectively	restrict	traffic	in	the	cluster.
rationale: Kubernetes	network	policies	are	enforced	by	the	CNI	plugin	in	use.	As	such	it	is	important to	ensure	that	the	CNI	plugin	supports	both	Ingress	and	Egress	network	policies. Impact: None
audit: Review	the	documentation	of	CNI	plugin	in	use	by	the	cluster,	and	confirm	that	it	supports Ingress	and	Egress	network	policies.
remediation: If	the	CNI	plugin	in	use	does	not	support	network	policies,	consideration	should	be	given	to making	use	of	a	different	plugin,	or	finding	an	alternate	mechanism	for	restricting	traffic	in the	Kubernetes	cluster. Default	Value: This	will	depend	on	the	CNI	plugin	in	use. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/extend-kubernetes/compute-storage- net/network-plugins/ Additional	Information: 232	|	P a g e One	example	here	is	Flannel	(https://github.com/coreos/flannel)	which	does	not	support Network	policy	unless	Calico	is	also	in	use. CIS	Controls: Version	6 9	Limitation	and	Control	of	Network	Ports,	Protocols,	and	Services Limitation	and	Control	of	Network	Ports,	Protocols,	and	Services Version	7 9	Limitation	and	Control	of	Network	Ports,	Protocols,	and	Services Limitation	and	Control	of	Network	Ports,	Protocols,	and	Services 233	|	P a g e

CIS	Controls: Version	6 9	Limitation	and	Control	of	Network	Ports,	Protocols,	and	Services Limitation	and	Control	of	Network	Ports,	Protocols,	and	Services Version	7 9	Limitation	and	Control	of	Network	Ports,	Protocols,	and	Services Limitation	and	Control	of	Network	Ports,	Protocols,	and	Services 233	|	P a g e
justification: While some aspects, such as identifying the CNI plugin in use, can be partially automated via Kubernetes API queries or Alibaba Cloud ACK APIs, the core requirement of this control is to confirm whether the CNI plugin supports both ingress and egress network policies. This information is not exposed via Alibaba Cloud APIs or standard Kubernetes API responses; it requires reviewing the plugin's official documentation or release notes. Therefore, a fully automated check is not feasible for this control.
manual_summary: 1. **Identify the CNI Plugin in Use:** - Access the Alibaba Cloud ACK Console for the target Kubernetes cluster. - Navigate to the cluster details and review the networking section to determine the CNI plugin deployed (e.g., Terway, Flannel, Calico). - Alternatively, use `kubectl get pods -n kube-system` and look for pods related to known CNI plugins (e.g., calico-node, flannel, terway-eni). 2. **Review Official Documentation:** - Locate the official documentation or GitHub repository for the identified CNI plugin. - Specifically search for sections on "Network Policy" support, ensuring both ingress and egress policies are mentioned as supported features. 3. **Verify Compliance:** - Confirm that the CNI plugin explicitly states support for both ingress and egress network policies. - Document the source (URL, documentation version) where this support is confirmed. 4. **Record Findings:** - Note the CNI plugin name, version, and documentation reference in your compliance records. - If the plugin does not support both policy types, flag as non-compliant and recommend remediation.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that all Namespaces have Network Policies defined
description: Kubernetes	supports	mounting	secrets	as	data	volumes	or	as	environment	variables. Minimize	the	use	of	environment	variable	secrets.
rationale: It	is	reasonably	common	for	application	code	to	log	out	its	environment	(particularly	in	the event	of	an	error).	This	will	include	any	secret	values	passed	in	as	environment	variables, so	secrets	can	easily	be	exposed	to	any	user	or	entity	who	has	access	to	the	logs. Impact: Application	code	which	expects	to	read	secrets	in	the	form	of	environment	variables	would need	modification
audit: Run	the	following	command	to	find	references	to	objects	which	use	environment	variables defined	from	secrets. kubectl get all -o jsonpath='{range .items[?(@..secretKeyRef)]} {.kind} {.metadata.name} {"\n"}{end}' -A
remediation: If	possible,	rewrite	application	code	to	read	secrets	from	mounted	secret	files,	rather	than from	environment	variables. Default	Value: By	default,	secrets	are	not	defined References: 236	|	P a g e 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/secret/#using-secrets Additional	Information: Mounting	secrets	as	volumes	has	the	additional	benefit	that	secret	values	can	be	updated without	restarting	the	pod CIS	Controls: Version	7 14.4	Encrypt	All	Sensitive	Information	in	Transit Encrypt	all	sensitive	information	in	transit. 14.8	Encrypt	Sensitive	Information	at	Rest Encrypt	all	sensitive	information	at	rest	using	a	tool	that	requires	a	secondary authentication	mechanism	not	integrated	into	the	operating	system,	in	order	to	access	the information. 237	|	P a g e
impact: Application	code	which	expects	to	read	secrets	in	the	form	of	environment	variables	would need	modification
references: 236	|	P a g e 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/secret/#using-secrets Additional	Information: Mounting	secrets	as	volumes	has	the	additional	benefit	that	secret	values	can	be	updated without	restarting	the	pod CIS	Controls: Version	7 14.4	Encrypt	All	Sensitive	Information	in	Transit Encrypt	all	sensitive	information	in	transit. 14.8	Encrypt	Sensitive	Information	at	Rest Encrypt	all	sensitive	information	at	rest	using	a	tool	that	requires	a	secondary authentication	mechanism	not	integrated	into	the	operating	system,	in	order	to	access	the information. 237	|	P a g e
justification: The audit requires verifying that all Kubernetes namespaces have network policies defined. This information is only accessible via the Kubernetes API (using kubectl or similar tools) and is not exposed through Alibaba Cloud APIs, Aliyun CLI, or Alibaba Cloud Config. Alibaba Cloud does not provide a native API or managed service to enumerate or validate Kubernetes network policies at the namespace level across ACK clusters. Therefore, this check cannot be fully automated using Alibaba Cloud’s native tools.
manual_summary: 1. **Access the Kubernetes Cluster:** - Use `kubectl` with appropriate credentials (kubeconfig) to connect to your ACK cluster. 2. **List Network Policies Across All Namespaces:** - Run: `kubectl get networkpolicies --all-namespaces` - This command lists all defined network policies in every namespace. 3. **Identify Namespaces Without Network Policies:** - Run: `kubectl get namespaces` to list all namespaces. - For each namespace, check if it appears in the output of the previous command. - Any namespace not listed does not have a network policy defined. 4. **Verify Compliance:** - Ensure every namespace has at least one network policy. - Document any namespace lacking a network policy as non-compliant. 5. **Expected Secure Configuration:** - All namespaces should have at least one network policy that restricts traffic according to the principle of least privilege.

description: Configure	Image	Provenance	for	your	deployment.

These provenance settings are cluster-level Kubernetes configurations, not Alibaba Cloud-managed resources, and require direct inspection of cluster YAMLs or admission controller setups. Therefore, a manual review is necessary to confirm compliance with this control.
manual_summary: 1. **Collect Pod Definitions:** - Access your Alibaba Cloud ACK (Kubernetes) cluster via `kubectl` or the Alibaba Cloud console. - Export all current pod definitions and relevant admission controller configurations (e.g., `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o yaml` and `kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations,mutatingwebhookconfigurations -o yaml`). 2. **Review Image Provenance Configuration:** - Examine the exported YAML files for evidence of image provenance enforcement, such as the use of `ImagePolicyWebhook` or other admission controllers that enforce image signature or provenance checks. - Confirm that these policies reference approved image registries, signatures, or CI/CD pipelines as required by your organization. 3. **Verify Consistency and Coverage:** - Ensure that image provenance policies are consistently applied across all namespaces and workloads. - Check that there are no exceptions or bypasses that would allow unapproved images. 4. **Expected Secure Configuration:** - Only images from trusted sources (e.g., signed images, approved registries) are permitted. - ImagePolicyWebhook or equivalent is enabled and configured according to organizational policy. 5. **Confirm Compliance:** - Document findings and retain evidence (YAML snippets, screenshots) showing that image provenance controls are in place and enforced.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Create administrative boundaries between resources using namespaces
description: Use	namespaces	to	isolate	your	Kubernetes	objects.
rationale: Limiting	the	scope	of	user	permissions	can	reduce	the	impact	of	mistakes	or	malicious activities.	A	Kubernetes	namespace	allows	you	to	partition	created	resources	into	logically named	groups.	Resources	created	in	one	namespace	can	be	hidden	from	other	namespaces. By	default,	each	resource	created	by	a	user	in	Kubernetes	cluster	runs	in	a	default namespace,	called	default.	You	can	create	additional	namespaces	and	attach	resources	and users	to	them.	You	can	use	Kubernetes	Authorization	plugins	to	create	policies	that segregate	access	to	namespace	resources	between	different	users. Impact: You	need	to	switch	between	namespaces	for	administration.
audit: Run	the	below	command	and	review	the	namespaces	created	in	the	cluster. kubectl get namespaces Ensure	that	these	namespaces	are	the	ones	you	need	and	are	adequately	administered	as per	your	requirements.
remediation: Follow	the	documentation	and	create	namespaces	for	objects	in	your	deployment	as	you need	them. 241	|	P a g e Default	Value: By	default,	Kubernetes	starts	with	two	initial	namespaces: 1. default	-	The	default	namespace	for	objects	with	no	other	namespace 2. kube-system	-	The	namespace	for	objects	created	by	the	Kubernetes	system References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with- objects/namespaces/ 2. http://blog.kubernetes.io/2016/08/security-best-practices-kubernetes- deployment.html CIS	Controls: Version	6 14	Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Version	7 14	Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know 242	|	P a g e
impact: You	need	to	switch	between	namespaces	for	administration.
references: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with- objects/namespaces/ 2. http://blog.kubernetes.io/2016/08/security-best-practices-kubernetes- deployment.html CIS	Controls: Version	6 14	Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Version	7 14	Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know 242	|	P a g e
justification: While the retrieval of Kubernetes namespaces (e.g., via `kubectl get namespaces` or Alibaba Cloud ACK APIs) can be automated, the core of this control is to ensure that namespaces are created and administered according to organizational policy and security requirements. This assessment requires human judgment to determine if the namespace structure aligns with business needs and security boundaries, which cannot be fully automated by any Alibaba Cloud API or CLI. Automation can only provide the raw data, not the compliance decision.
manual_summary: 1. Use the Alibaba Cloud ACK Console or connect to your cluster using `kubectl`: - In terminal: Run `kubectl get namespaces` to list all namespaces in the cluster. - Or, navigate to the Alibaba Cloud ACK Console > Clusters > [Your Cluster] > Namespaces. 2. Review the list of namespaces and compare them against your organization's documented namespace policy and security requirements. 3. Verify that each namespace serves a clear, documented purpose and that resource isolation is enforced as intended (e.g., no unnecessary or unused namespaces, no policy violations). 4. Confirm that namespace usage aligns with security best practices for access separation and resource isolation. 5. Document your findings and confirm compliance or note any deviations for remediation.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the seccomp profile is set to runtime/default in your pod definitions
description: Enable	runtime/default	seccomp	profile	in	your	pod	definitions.
rationale: Seccomp	(secure	computing	mode)	is	used	to	restrict	the	set	of	system	calls	applications can	make,	allowing	cluster	administrators	greater	control	over	the	security	of	workloads running	in	the	cluster.	Kubernetes	disables	seccomp	profiles	by	default	for	historical reasons.	You	should	enable	it	to	ensure	that	the	workloads	have	restricted	actions	available within	the	container. Impact: If	the	runtime/default	seccomp	profile	is	too	restrictive	for	you,	you	would	have	to create/manage	your	own	seccomp	profiles.	Also,	you	need	to	enable	all	alpha	features	for this	to	work.	There	is	no	individual	switch	to	turn	on	this	feature.
audit: Review	the	pod	definitions	in	your	cluster.	It	should	create	a	line	as	below: annotations: seccomp.security.alpha.kubernetes.io/pod: runtime/default
remediation: Seccomp	is	an	alpha	feature	currently.	By	default,	all	alpha	features	are	disabled.	So,	you would	need	to	enable	alpha	features	in	the	apiserver	by	passing	"--feature- gates=AllAlpha=true"	argument. Edit	the	/etc/kubernetes/apiserver	file	on	the	master	node	and	set	the	KUBE_API_ARGS parameter	to	"--feature-gates=AllAlpha=true" KUBE_API_ARGS="--feature-gates=AllAlpha=true" Based	on	your	system,	restart	the	kube-apiserver	service.	For	example: 243	|	P a g e systemctl restart kube-apiserver.service Use	annotations	to	enable	the	docker/default	seccomp	profile	in	your	pod	definitions.	An example	is	as	below: apiVersion: v1 kind: Pod metadata: name: trustworthy-pod annotations: seccomp.security.alpha.kubernetes.io/pod: runtime/default spec: containers: - name: trustworthy-container image: sotrustworthy:latest Default	Value: By	default,	seccomp	profile	is	set	to	unconfined	which	means	that	no	seccomp	profiles	are enabled. References: 1. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/39845 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/21790 3. https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/design- proposals/seccomp.md#examples 4. https://docs.docker.com/engine/security/seccomp/ 5. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tutorials/clusters/seccomp/ CIS	Controls: Version	6 5	Controlled	Use	of	Administration	Privileges Controlled	Use	of	Administration	Privileges Version	7 5.1	Establish	Secure	Configurations Maintain	documented,	standard	security	configuration	standards	for	all	authorized operating	systems	and	software. 244	|	P a g e
impact: If	the	runtime/default	seccomp	profile	is	too	restrictive	for	you,	you	would	have	to create/manage	your	own	seccomp	profiles.	Also,	you	need	to	enable	all	alpha	features	for this	to	work.	There	is	no	individual	switch	to	turn	on	this	feature.
references: 1. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/39845 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/21790 3. https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/design- proposals/seccomp.md#examples 4. https://docs.docker.com/engine/security/seccomp/ 5. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tutorials/clusters/seccomp/ CIS	Controls: Version	6 5	Controlled	Use	of	Administration	Privileges Controlled	Use	of	Administration	Privileges Version	7 5.1	Establish	Secure	Configurations Maintain	documented,	standard	security	configuration	standards	for	all	authorized operating	systems	and	software. 244	|	P a g e
justification: While the first opinion suggests manual inspection, the second correctly points out that the verification of seccomp profile annotations in pod definitions can be fully automated using Kubernetes-native commands and scripting. Alibaba Cloud ACK clusters expose Kubernetes APIs, and `kubectl` can be used programmatically to extract and parse pod definitions. Although Alibaba Cloud's own CLI does not natively expose seccomp profile status, automation is feasible by scripting against the Kubernetes API, which is the authoritative source for this configuration.
automation_summary: - Alibaba Cloud Resource/Service: ACK (Alibaba Cloud Container Service for Kubernetes) Pods - API/CLI Method: Use `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json` (or `-o yaml`) to retrieve all pod definitions from the cluster. - Properties to Check: - The presence and value of the annotation: `seccomp.security.alpha.kubernetes.io/pod` - Value must be exactly: `runtime/default` - Pass Criteria: - Every pod in the cluster has the annotation `seccomp.security.alpha.kubernetes.io/pod` set to `runtime/default`. - Fail Criteria: - Any pod is missing the annotation, or the annotation value is not `runtime/default`.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Apply Security Context to Your Pods and Containers
description: Apply	Security	Context	to	Your	Pods	and	Containers
rationale: A	security	context	defines	the	operating	system	security	settings	(uid,	gid,	capabilities, SELinux	role,	etc..)	applied	to	a	container.	When	designing	your	containers	and	pods,	make sure	that	you	configure	the	security	context	for	your	pods,	containers,	and	volumes.	A security	context	is	a	property	defined	in	the	deployment	yaml.	It	controls	the	security parameters	that	will	be	assigned	to	the	pod/container/volume.	There	are	two	levels	of security	context:	pod	level	security	context,	and	container	level	security	context. Impact: If	you	incorrectly	apply	security	contexts,	you	may	have	trouble	running	the	pods.
audit: Review	the	pod	definitions	in	your	cluster	and	verify	that	you	have	security	contexts defined	as	appropriate.
remediation: Follow	the	Kubernetes	documentation	and	apply	security	contexts	to	your	pods.	For	a suggested	list	of	security	contexts,	you	may	refer	to	the	CIS	Security	Benchmark	for	Docker Containers. Default	Value: By	default,	no	security	contexts	are	automatically	applied	to	pods. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/policy/security-context/ 2. https://learn.cisecurity.org/benchmarks CIS	Controls: 245	|	P a g e Version	6 3	Secure	Configurations	for	Hardware	and	Software	on	Mobile	Devices,	Laptops, Workstations,	and	Servers Secure	Configurations	for	Hardware	and	Software	on	Mobile	Devices,	Laptops, Workstations,	and	Servers Version	7 5.1	Establish	Secure	Configurations Maintain	documented,	standard	security	configuration	standards	for	all	authorized operating	systems	and	software. 246	|	P a g e
impact: If	you	incorrectly	apply	security	contexts,	you	may	have	trouble	running	the	pods.
references: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/policy/security-context/ 2. https://learn.cisecurity.org/benchmarks CIS	Controls: 245	|	P a g e Version	6 3	Secure	Configurations	for	Hardware	and	Software	on	Mobile	Devices,	Laptops, Workstations,	and	Servers Secure	Configurations	for	Hardware	and	Software	on	Mobile	Devices,	Laptops, Workstations,	and	Servers Version	7 5.1	Establish	Secure	Configurations Maintain	documented,	standard	security	configuration	standards	for	all	authorized operating	systems	and	software. 246	|	P a g e
justification: While human judgment is required to assess the appropriateness of specific security context values, the presence or absence of a `securityContext` in pod and container specifications can be programmatically checked using Alibaba Cloud's Kubernetes APIs or via `kubectl` commands. This enables automated detection of whether security contexts are defined, which directly addresses the audit step of verifying their existence. Full compliance evaluation may require manual review, but the control as written focuses on the existence of security contexts, which is automatable.
automation_summary: - Alibaba Cloud Resource/Service: Container Service for Kubernetes (ACK) Pods - API/CLI Method: - Use `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o yaml` or Alibaba Cloud Kubernetes API to retrieve all pod specifications. - Properties to Check: - For each pod and its containers, check for the presence of the `securityContext` field in both `spec.securityContext` (pod-level) and `spec.containers[].securityContext` (container-level). - Pass Criteria: - Every pod and every container within each pod has a `securityContext` field defined (even if empty, but ideally with at least one security-relevant parameter set). - Fail Criteria: - Any pod or container is missing the `securityContext` field in its specification.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: The default namespace should not be used
description: Kubernetes	provides	a	default	namespace,	where	objects	are	placed	if	no	namespace	is specified	for	them.	Placing	objects	in	this	namespace	makes	application	of	RBAC	and	other controls	more	difficult.
rationale: Resources	in	a	Kubernetes	cluster	should	be	segregated	by	namespace,	to	allow	for	security controls	to	be	applied	at	that	level	and	to	make	it	easier	to	manage	resources. Impact: None
audit: Run	this	command	to	list	objects	in	default	namespace kubectl get all The	only	entries	there	should	be	system	managed	resources	such	as	the	kubernetes	service
remediation: Ensure	that	namespaces	are	created	to	allow	for	appropriate	segregation	of	Kubernetes resources	and	that	all	new	resources	are	created	in	a	specific	namespace. Default	Value: Unless	a	namespace	is	specific	on	object	creation,	the	default	namespace	will	be	used CIS	Controls: Version	6 5.1	Minimize	And	Sparingly	Use	Administrative	Privileges Minimize	administrative	privileges	and	only	use	administrative	accounts	when	they	are 247	|	P a g e required.	Implement	focused	auditing	on	the	use	of	administrative	privileged	functions	and monitor	for	anomalous	behavior. Version	7 5.1	Establish	Secure	Configurations Maintain	documented,	standard	security	configuration	standards	for	all	authorized operating	systems	and	software. 6 Managed services This	section	consists	of	security	recommendations	for	the	direct	configuration	of Kubernetes	managed	service	components,	namely,	Alibaba	Cloud	Container	Service	for Kubernetes	(ACK).	These	recommendations	are	directly	applicable	for	features	which	exist only	as	part	of	a	managed	service. 248	|	P a g e 6.1 Image Registry and Image Scanning This	section	contains	recommendations	relating	to	container	image	registries	and	securing images	in	those	registries,	such	as	Alibaba	Cloud	Container	Registry	(ACR).
justification: While Alibaba Cloud APIs and CLI tools (such as Aliyun CLI and `kubectl`) can automate the retrieval of all resources in the default namespace, the critical compliance determination—distinguishing between system-managed and user-created resources—requires human judgment and contextual understanding. There is no definitive API property or automated logic that can reliably classify every resource as "system" or "non-system" without manual review, especially as system resources may vary by cluster version and configuration.
manual_summary: 1. Ensure you have access to the target Alibaba Cloud ACK cluster and that `kubectl` is configured with appropriate credentials. 2. Run the following command to list all resources in the default namespace: ``` kubectl get all --namespace=default ``` 3. Review the output and identify all resources present. 4. Compare the listed resources against the expected system-managed resources (e.g., the default `kubernetes` service). 5. Confirm that no user-created workloads (such as Deployments, Pods, Services, etc.) are present in the default namespace. 6. Document any non-system resources found and escalate for remediation. 7. Mark the control as compliant only if the default namespace contains exclusively system-managed resources.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0

Configure	image	security	scanning	rule Default	Value: 249	|	P a g e By	default,	ACR	Container	Analysis	is	disabled. justification: Both AI opinions correctly identified that Alibaba Cloud Container Registry (ACR) exposes vulnerability scanning configuration and status via API and CLI, making automated verification feasible. Alibaba Cloud provides specific API endpoints and CLI commands (e.g., `aliyun cr DescribeRepositoryList`, `aliyun cr DescribeRepoSyncTaskList`, or similar) that allow programmatic checking of whether image vulnerability scanning is enabled for each repository. automation_summary: - Alibaba Cloud Resource/Service: Container Registry (ACR) Repositories - API/CLI Method: - List repositories: `aliyun cr DescribeRepositoryList --region <region>` - Check scanning configuration: Use `aliyun cr DescribeRepoSyncTaskList --RepoId <repo_id>` or equivalent API to verify if vulnerability scanning tasks are configured and active. - Properties to Check: - Existence and status of vulnerability scanning rules/tasks for each repository (e.g., `SyncTaskType: SecurityScan`, `Status: Enabled` or similar fields). - Pass Criteria: - All repositories have image vulnerability scanning enabled (i.e., at least one active security scan task per repository). - Fail Criteria: - Any repository is missing an active vulnerability scanning configuration or the scanning task is disabled.

justification: Both AI opinions correctly identify that Alibaba Cloud exposes all necessary information for auditing ACR access controls via APIs and CLI. RAM policies and network access controls for ACR can be programmatically listed and evaluated using Alibaba Cloud’s RAM and ACR APIs, including checks for policy scope and VPC endpoint restrictions. automation_summary: - Alibaba Cloud Resource/Service: ACR Instances, RAM Policies, VPC Endpoints/Security Groups - API/CLI Methods: - `aliyun acr ListInstances` (to enumerate ACR instances) - `aliyun ram ListPolicies` and `aliyun ram GetPolicy` (to enumerate and inspect RAM policies attached to users/groups/roles with ACR permissions) - `aliyun acr DescribeInstanceVpcEndpoint` (to verify VPC endpoint/network access restrictions for each ACR instance) - `aliyun ecs DescribeSecurityGroups` and `aliyun ecs DescribeSecurityGroupAttribute` (to check security group rules if public access is enabled) - Properties to Check: - RAM policy statements granting ACR access (ensure only authorized users/service accounts have permissions such as `acr:PullRepository`, `acr:PushRepository`, etc.) - ACR instance network access: confirm VPC endpoint is enabled or public access is restricted by IP/CIDR/security group - Pass Criteria: - Only authorized users/service accounts have ACR access via RAM policies - ACR instances are not publicly accessible, or if public, access is tightly restricted by IP/CIDR/security group - Fail Criteria: - Broad or wildcard RAM policies granting ACR access to unauthorized users - ACR instances with unrestricted public access or overly permissive network controls

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Minimize cluster access to read-only for ACR
description: Configure	the	Cluster	Service	Account	with	Storage	Object	Viewer	Role	to	only	allow	read- only	access	to	ACR.

Restricting	permissions	follows	the	principles of	least	privilege	and	prevents	credentials	from	being	abused	beyond	the	required	role. audit: Review	Alibaba	Cloud	ECS	master	node	and	worker	node	RAM	roles'	RAM	Policy Permissions	to	verify	that	they	are	set	and	the	minimum	required	level. Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console 1. Go	to	ACK	by	visiting	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/ 2. In	the	left-side	navigation	pane,	click Clusters . 3. On	the Clusters page,	click	the	name	of	a	cluster	or	click Details in	the	Actions column. On	the Cluster	Information page,	click	the Cluster	Resources tab	and	click	the name	of	the**	worker	RAM	role**	or master	RAM	role on	the	tab. 6. You	are	then	redirected	to	the	RAM	console. source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure Kubernetes Secrets are encrypted using keys managed in KMS
description: Encrypt	Kubernetes	secrets,	stored	in	etcd,	at	the	application-layer	using	a	customer- managed	key	in	Cloud	KMS.
rationale: Kubernetes	can	store	secrets	that	pods	can	access	via	a	mounted	volume.	Today, Kubernetes	secrets	are	stored	with	Base64	encoding,	but	encrypting	is	the	recommended approach.	ACK	Pro	clusters	support	the	capability	of	encrypting	your	Kubernetes	secrets using	Key	Management	Service	(KMS)	Customer	Managed	Keys	(CMK).	The	only requirement	is	to	enable	the	encryption	provider	support	during	ACK	cluster	creation. Use	Key	Management	Service	(KMS)	keys	to	provide	envelope	encryption	of	Kubernetes secrets	stored	in	ACK.	Implementing	envelope	encryption	is	considered	a	security	best practice	for	applications	that	store	sensitive	data	and	is	part	of	a	defense	in	depth	security strategy. Application-layer	Secrets	Encryption	provides	an	additional	layer	of	security	for	sensitive data,	such	as	user	defined	Secrets	and	Secrets	required	for	the	operation	of	the	cluster, such	as	service	account	keys,	which	are	all	stored	in	etcd. Using	this	functionality,	you	can	use	a	key,	that	you	manage	in	KMS,	to	encrypt	data	at	the application	layer.	This	protects	against	attackers	in	the	event	that	they	manage	to	gain access	to	etcd.
audit: Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console 1. Go	to	ACK	by	visiting	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/ 256	|	P a g e 2. Select	the	desired	cluster,	and	within	the	'Basic	Information'	pane,	make	sure	the 'Secret	Encryption'	is	enabled.
remediation: Enable	'Secret	Encryption'	during	ACK	Pro	cluster	creation	as	described	in	the	links	within the	'References'	section. Default	Value: By	default,	Application-layer	Secrets	Encryption	is	not	enabled. References: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/177372.htm Additional	Information: This	control	only	available	for	ACK	Pro	clusters CIS	Controls: Version	7 14.8	Encrypt	Sensitive	Information	at	Rest Encrypt	all	sensitive	information	at	rest	using	a	tool	that	requires	a	secondary authentication	mechanism	not	integrated	into	the	operating	system,	in	order	to	access	the information. 257	|	P a g e 6.3 Cluster Networking This	section	contains	recommendations	relating	to	network	security	configurations	in	ACK.
references: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/177372.htm Additional	Information: This	control	only	available	for	ACK	Pro	clusters CIS	Controls: Version	7 14.8	Encrypt	Sensitive	Information	at	Rest Encrypt	all	sensitive	information	at	rest	using	a	tool	that	requires	a	secondary authentication	mechanism	not	integrated	into	the	operating	system,	in	order	to	access	the information. 257	|	P a g e 6.3 Cluster Networking This	section	contains	recommendations	relating	to	network	security	configurations	in	ACK.
justification: Both AI opinions correctly identified that the encryption status of Kubernetes Secrets in ACK Pro clusters can be programmatically checked using Alibaba Cloud APIs, specifically via the `DescribeClusterDetail` API. The required property (`secretEncryption`) is exposed in the cluster configuration, making automation feasible and reliable. Manual steps are unnecessary since all required information is available through standard API calls.
automation_summary: - Alibaba Cloud Resource/Service: Alibaba Cloud Container Service for Kubernetes (ACK) Pro Clusters - API/CLI Method: Aliyun CLI/SDK – `cs.DescribeClusterDetail` (or equivalent SDK call) - Properties to Check: - `secretEncryption.enabled` (should be `true`) - `secretEncryption.kmsKeyId` (should be present and reference a customer-managed KMS key) - Pass Criteria: - The cluster’s `secretEncryption.enabled` property is `true` AND - `secretEncryption.kmsKeyId` is set to a valid KMS CMK - Fail Criteria: - `secretEncryption.enabled` is `false` or missing, OR - `secretEncryption.kmsKeyId` is missing or not referencing a customer-managed KMS key

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Restrict Access to the Control Plane Endpoint
description: Enable	Endpoint	Private	Access	to	restrict	access	to	the	cluster's	control	plane	to	only	an allowlist	of	authorized	IPs.
rationale: Authorized	networks	are	a	way	of	specifying	a	restricted	range	of	IP	addresses	that	are permitted	to	access	your	cluster's	control	plane.	Kubernetes	Engine	uses	both	Transport Layer	Security	(TLS)	and	authentication	to	provide	secure	access	to	your	cluster's	control plane	from	the	public	internet.	This	provides	you	the	flexibility	to	administer	your	cluster from	anywhere;	however,	you	might	want	to	further	restrict	access	to	a	set	of	IP	addresses that	you	control.	You	can	set	this	restriction	by	specifying	an	authorized	network. Restricting	access	to	an	authorized	network	can	provide	additional	security	benefits	for your	container	cluster,	including: • Better	protection	from	outsider	attacks:	Authorized	networks	provide	an	additional layer	of	security	by	limiting	external	access	to	a	specific	set	of	addresses	you designate,	such	as	those	that	originate	from	your	premises.	This	helps	protect	access to	your	cluster	in	the	case	of	a	vulnerability	in	the	cluster's	authentication	or authorization	mechanism. • Better	protection	from	insider	attacks:	Authorized	networks	help	protect	your cluster	from	accidental	leaks	of	master	certificates	from	your	company's	premises. Leaked	certificates	used	from	outside	ECS	and	outside	the	authorized	IP	ranges	(for example,	from	addresses	outside	your	company)	are	still	denied	access Impact: When	implementing	Endpoint	Private	Access,	be	careful	to	ensure	all	desired	networks	are on	the	allowlist	(whitelist)	to	prevent	inadvertently	blocking	external	access	to	your cluster's	control	plane.
audit: 258	|	P a g e Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console: 1. Go	to	ACK	by	visiting	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/ 2. From	the	list	of	clusters,	click	on	the	desired	cluster	to	open	the	Details	page.	Under the	'Basic	Information'	section,	click	'Set	access	control'	link	to	view	access	control of	control	plane	endpoint.
remediation: For	exist	cluster: Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console 1. Go	to	ACK	by	visiting	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/ 2. When	create	cluster,	make	sure	not	checked	the	'Public	Access'	checkbox. Default	Value: By	default,	Endpoint	Private	Access	is	disabled. These	controls	will	enforce	the principle	that	only	authorized	individuals	should	have	access	to	the	information	based	on their	need	to	access	the	information	as	a	part	of	their	responsibilities. 259	|	P a g e
impact: When	implementing	Endpoint	Private	Access,	be	careful	to	ensure	all	desired	networks	are on	the	allowlist	(whitelist)	to	prevent	inadvertently	blocking	external	access	to	your cluster's	control	plane.

These	controls	will	enforce	the principle	that	only	authorized	individuals	should	have	access	to	the	information	based	on their	need	to	access	the	information	as	a	part	of	their	responsibilities. 259	|	P a g e
justification: Both AI opinions correctly identify that the control plane endpoint access configuration for Alibaba Cloud ACK clusters is fully exposed via the Alibaba Cloud API and Aliyun CLI. The `DescribeClusterDetail` API provides all necessary details to programmatically determine if private access is enabled and if an allowlist of authorized IPs is configured. This makes the audit fully automatable without requiring manual console inspection.
automation_summary: - Alibaba Cloud Resource/Service: ACK (Alibaba Cloud Container Service for Kubernetes) Clusters - API/CLI Method: - API: `DescribeClusterDetail` - CLI: `aliyun cs DescribeClusterDetail --ClusterId <cluster_id>` - Properties to Check: - `api_server_public_access` (should be false or not enabled) - `api_server_authorized_ip_ranges` (should exist and contain only authorized/allowlisted IPs) - `endpoint_public_access_enabled` (should be false, if present) - Pass Criteria: - Control plane endpoint does NOT allow public access (`api_server_public_access` is false or not present) - An allowlist of authorized IPs is set (`api_server_authorized_ip_ranges` is present and contains only trusted IPs) - Fail Criteria: - Control plane endpoint allows public access (`api_server_public_access` is true or `endpoint_public_access_enabled` is true) - No allowlist is configured, or the allowlist contains `0.0.0.0/0` or other broad/untrusted ranges

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure clusters are created with Private Endpoint Enabled and Public Access Disabled
description: Disable	access	to	the	Kubernetes	API	from	outside	the	node	network	if	it	is	not	required.
rationale: In	a	private	cluster,	the	master	node	has	two	endpoints,	a	private	and	public	endpoint.	The private	endpoint	is	the	internal	IP	address	of	the	master,	behind	an	internal	load	balancer in	the	master's	VPC	network.	Nodes	communicate	with	the	master	using	the	private endpoint.	The	public	endpoint	enables	the	Kubernetes	API	to	be	accessed	from	outside	the master's	VPC	network. Although	Kubernetes	API	requires	an	authorized	token	to	perform	sensitive	actions,	a vulnerability	could	potentially	expose	the	Kubernetes	publically	with	unrestricted	access. Additionally,	an	attacker	may	be	able	to	identify	the	current	cluster	and	Kubernetes	API version	and	determine	whether	it	is	vulnerable	to	an	attack.	Unless	required,	disabling public	endpoint	will	help	prevent	such	threats,	and	require	the	attacker	to	be	on	the master's	VPC	network	to	perform	any	attack	on	the	Kubernetes	API. Impact: Configure	the	ACK	cluster	endpoint	to	be	private. 1. Leave	the	cluster	endpoint	public	and	specify	which	CIDR	blocks	can	communicate with	the	cluster	endpoint.	The	blocks	are	effectively	a	whitelisted	set	of	public	IP addresses	that	are	allowed	to	access	the	cluster	endpoint. 2. Disable	access	to	the	cluster	endpoint	from	outside	the	cluster	VPC. Audit: Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console 1. Go	to	ACK	by	visiting	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/ 2. Select	the	desired	cluster,	and	within	the	'Cluster	Resources'	pane,	click	on	'SLB'	link 3. Within	the	SLB	page,	make	sure	the	SLB	not	bind	an	EIP

On	the	SLB	page,	click	on	'Unbind	EIP'	button	to	unbind	EIP For	create	cluster: Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console 1. When	create	cluster,	make	sure	not	checked	the	'Public	Access'	checkbox. Default	Value: By	default,	the	Private	Endpoint	is	disabled. The	blocks	are	effectively	a	whitelisted	set	of	public	IP addresses	that	are	allowed	to	access	the	cluster	endpoint. 2. Disable	access	to	the	cluster	endpoint	from	outside	the	cluster	VPC. Audit: Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console 1. Go	to	ACK	by	visiting	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/ 2. Select	the	desired	cluster,	and	within	the	'Cluster	Resources'	pane,	click	on	'SLB'	link 3. Within	the	SLB	page,	make	sure	the	SLB	not	bind	an	EIP
references: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/95108.htm CIS	Controls: Version	7 12	Boundary	Defense Boundary	Defense 261	|	P a g e
justification: Both AI opinions correctly identified that Alibaba Cloud exposes the necessary cluster endpoint configuration (private/public access) via the `cs.DescribeClusterDetail` API and Aliyun CLI, making this control fully automatable. The relevant properties (`privateEndpoint`, `publicAccess`) are directly retrievable and verifiable without manual console inspection. No evidence suggests any required attribute is hidden from API/CLI access.
automation_summary: - Alibaba Cloud Resource/Service: Alibaba Cloud Container Service for Kubernetes (ACK) Clusters - API/CLI Method: - API: `cs.DescribeClusterDetail` - CLI: `aliyun cs DescribeClusterDetail --ClusterId <cluster-id>` - Properties to Check: - `privateEndpoint` (should be `true` or enabled) - `publicAccess` (should be `false` or not enabled) - Pass Criteria: - `privateEndpoint` is enabled (true) - `publicAccess` is disabled (false) - Fail Criteria: - `privateEndpoint` is not enabled (false) - OR `publicAccess` is enabled (true)

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure clusters are created with Private Nodes
description: Disable	public	IP	addresses	for	cluster	nodes,	so	that	they	only	have	private	IP	addresses. Private	Nodes	are	nodes	with	no	public	IP	addresses.
rationale: Disabling	public	IP	addresses	on	cluster	nodes	restricts	access	to	only	internal	networks, forcing	attackers	to	obtain	local	network	access	before	attempting	to	compromise	the underlying	Kubernetes	hosts. Impact: To	enable	Private	Nodes,	the	cluster	has	to	also	be	configured	with	a	private	master	IP range	and	IP	Aliasing	enabled. Private	Nodes	do	not	have	outbound	access	to	the	public	internet.	If	you	want	to	provide outbound	Internet	access	for	your	private	nodes,	you	can	use	Cloud	NAT	or	you	can manage	your	own	NAT	gateway.
audit: Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console 1. Go	to	ACK	by	visiting	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/ 2. Select	the	desired	cluster,	and	within	the	'Cluster	Resources'	pane,	click	on	'NAT Gateway'	link. 3. On	NAT	gateway	page,	make	sure	no	NAT	gateway	available	or	not	exist	any	SNAT entriy.
remediation: For	exist	cluster: Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console 1. Go	to	ACK	by	visiting	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/ 2. Select	the	desired	cluster,	and	within	the	'Cluster	Resources'	pane,	click	on	'NAT Gateway'	link. 3. On	NAT	gateway	page,	delete	any	exist	SNAT	entries. 262	|	P a g e For	create	cluster: Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console 1. When	create	cluster,	make	sure	not	checked	the	'Configure	SNAT'	checkbox. If	you	want	to	provide outbound	Internet	access	for	your	private	nodes,	you	can	use	Cloud	NAT	or	you	can manage	your	own	NAT	gateway.
references: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/95108.htm 2. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/121157.html CIS	Controls: Version	7 12	Boundary	Defense Boundary	Defense 263	|	P a g e
justification: Both AI opinions correctly identify that Alibaba Cloud APIs and CLI commands can be used to programmatically determine whether ACK cluster nodes have public IP addresses and whether SNAT entries exist, which would violate the "private nodes only" requirement. The necessary data (node public IP assignments and SNAT table entries) are accessible via the Aliyun CLI (`cs DescribeClusterNodes`, `vpc DescribeSnatTableEntries`), making full automation feasible and reliable for compliance checks.
automation_summary: - Alibaba Cloud Resource/Service: ACK (Alibaba Cloud Container Service for Kubernetes) clusters and VPC SNAT tables - API/CLI Method: - List clusters: `aliyun cs DescribeClusters` - For each cluster, list nodes: `aliyun cs DescribeClusterNodes --ClusterId <cluster_id>` - For each node, check for presence of public IP addresses in the node details - List SNAT entries for the cluster's VPC: `aliyun vpc DescribeSnatTableEntries --RegionId <region_id>` - Properties to Check: - Node objects: `publicIpAddress` or equivalent field should be empty or null - SNAT table entries: No SNAT entry should exist that maps cluster node subnets to public IPs - Pass Criteria: - All nodes in every ACK cluster have only private IP addresses (no public IPs assigned) - No SNAT entries exist that provide outbound internet access for cluster nodes - Fail Criteria: - Any node in any ACK cluster has a public IP address assigned - Any SNAT entry exists that enables outbound internet access for cluster nodes

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure Network Policy is Enabled and set as appropriate
description: Use	Network	Policy	to	restrict	pod	to	pod	traffic	within	a	cluster	and	segregate	workloads.
rationale: By	default,	all	pod	to	pod	traffic	within	a	cluster	is	allowed.	Network	Policy	creates	a podlevel	firewall	that	can	be	used	to	restrict	traffic	between	sources.	Pod	traffic	is restricted	by	having	a	Network	Policy	that	selects	it	(through	the	use	of	labels).	Once	there is	any	Network	Policy	in	a	namespace	selecting	a	particular	pod,	that	pod	will	reject	any connections	that	are	not	allowed	by	any	Network	Policy.	Other	pods	in	the	namespace	that are	not	selected	by	any	Network	Policy	will	continue	to	accept	all	traffic. Network	Policies	are	managed	via	the	Kubernetes	Network	Policy	API	and	enforced	by	a network	plugin,	simply	creating	the	resource	without	a	compatible	network	plugin	to implement	it	will	have	no	effect.	ACK	supports	Network	Policy	enforcement	through	the use	of	Terway. Impact: Network	Policy	requires	the	Network	Policy	add-on.	This	add-on	is	included	automatically when	a	cluster	with	Network	Policy	is	created,	but	for	an	existing	cluster,	needs	to	be added	prior	to	enabling	Network	Policy
audit: Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console 1. Go	to	ACK	by	visiting	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/ 2. Select	the	desired	cluster,	and	within	the	Details	pane	make	sure	that	value	of 'Network	Plug-in'	is	'Terway
remediation: Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console 1. When	create	cluster,	make	sure	selected	use	'Terway'	as	'Network	Plug-in'	and checked	the	'Support	for	NetworkPolicy'	checkbox 264	|	P a g e Default	Value: By	default,	Network	Policy	is	disabled. description: Send	cluster	audit	logs	to	a	remote	aggregator	to	mitigate	the	risk	of	local	tampering	in	the event	of	a	breach.
rationale: Exporting	cluster	audit	logs	to	a	dedicated,	persistent	datastore	such	as	Log	Service	(SLS) ensures	availability	of	audit	data	following	a	cluster	security	event,	and	provides	a	central location	for	analysis	of	log	and	metric	data	collated	from	multiple	sources.
audit: Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console 1. Go	to	ACK	by	visiting	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/ 2. Select	the	desired	cluster,	and	within	the	'Security	>	Cluster	Auditing'	pane,	make sure	the	auditing	dashboard	is	enabled
remediation: Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console 1. Go	to	ACK	by	visiting	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/ 2. Select	the	desired	cluster,	and	within	the	'Security	>	Cluster	Auditing'	pane,	click	on 'Enable	Cluster	Auditing	Now'	button	to	enable	Cluster	Auditing. Default	Value: By	default,	cluster	auditing	is	disabled. automation_summary: - Alibaba Cloud Resource/Service: ACK (Alibaba Cloud Container Service for Kubernetes) Clusters - API/CLI Method: - Aliyun CLI: `aliyun cs DescribeClusterDetail --ClusterId <cluster-id>` - Alternatively, use the SDK method: `DescribeClusterDetail` - Properties to Check: - Look for the `auditLog` or equivalent field in the cluster details, specifically: - `auditLog.enabled` (should be `true`) - `auditLog.logProject` and `auditLog.logStore` (should be set, indicating logs are sent to Log Service/SLS) - Pass Criteria: - `auditLog.enabled` is `true` - `auditLog.logProject` and `auditLog.logStore` are non-empty (logs are sent to a remote aggregator) - Fail Criteria: - `auditLog.enabled` is `false` or missing - `auditLog.logProject` or `auditLog.logStore` are empty or missing (logs not sent to remote aggregator)

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure Pod Security Policy is Enabled and set as appropriate
description: Pod	Security	Policy	should	be	used	to	prevent	privileged	containers	where	possible	and enforce	namespace	and	workload	configurations.
rationale: A	Pod	Security	Policy	is	a	cluster-level	resource	that	controls	security	sensitive	aspects	of the	pod	specification.	A	PodSecurityPolicy	object	defines	a	set	of	conditions	that	a	pod must	run	with	in	order	to	be	accepted	into	the	system,	as	well	as	defaults	for	the	related fields.	When	a	request	to	create	or	update	a	Pod	does	not	meet	the	conditions	in	the	Pod Security	Policy,	that	request	is	rejected	and	an	error	is	returned.	The	Pod	Security	Policy admission	controller	validates	requests	against	available	Pod	Security	Policies. PodSecurityPolicies	specify	a	list	of	restrictions,	requirements,	and	defaults	for	Pods created	under	the	policy.	See	further	details	on	recommended	policies	in	Recommendation section	5.2. Impact: If	you	enable	the	Pod	Security	Policy	controller	without	first	defining	and	authorizing	any actual	policies,	no	users,	controllers,	or	service	accounts	can	create	or	update	Pods.	If	you are	working	with	an	existing	cluster,	you	should	define	and	authorize	policies	before enabling	the	controller.
audit: Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console 1. Go	to	ACK	by	visiting	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/ 271	|	P a g e 2. Select	the	desired	cluster,	and	within	the	'Security	>	PodSecurityPolicies'	pane, make	sure	the	PSP	State	is	'enabled'	and	there	are	many	policies	defined.
remediation: Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console 1. Go	to	ACK	by	visiting	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/ 2. Select	the	desired	cluster,	and	within	the	'Security	>	PodSecurityPolicies'	pane,	click on	PSP	State	button	to	enable	PSP. 3. Follow	the	ACK	document	to	setup	recommended	policies	in	Recommendation section	5.2: https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/173620.htm Default	Value: By	default,	Pod	Security	Policy	is	disabled. References: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/173620.htm 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/policy/pod-security-policy Additional	Information: Pod	Security	Policy	is	Beta	in	Kubernetes	and	in	ACK.	This	feature	is	not	covered	by	any SLA	or	deprecation	policy	and	might	be	subject	to	backward-incompatible	changes. CIS	Controls: Version	7 5.1	Establish	Secure	Configurations Maintain	documented,	standard	security	configuration	standards	for	all	authorized operating	systems	and	software. 5.4	Deploy	System	Configuration	Management	Tools Deploy	system	configuration	management	tools	that	will	automatically	enforce	and redeploy	configuration	settings	to	systems	at	regularly	scheduled	intervals. 272	|	P a g e
impact: If	you	enable	the	Pod	Security	Policy	controller	without	first	defining	and	authorizing	any actual	policies,	no	users,	controllers,	or	service	accounts	can	create	or	update	Pods.	If	you are	working	with	an	existing	cluster,	you	should	define	and	authorize	policies	before enabling	the	controller.
references: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/173620.htm 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/policy/pod-security-policy Additional	Information: Pod	Security	Policy	is	Beta	in	Kubernetes	and	in	ACK.	This	feature	is	not	covered	by	any SLA	or	deprecation	policy	and	might	be	subject	to	backward-incompatible	changes. CIS	Controls: Version	7 5.1	Establish	Secure	Configurations Maintain	documented,	standard	security	configuration	standards	for	all	authorized operating	systems	and	software. 5.4	Deploy	System	Configuration	Management	Tools Deploy	system	configuration	management	tools	that	will	automatically	enforce	and redeploy	configuration	settings	to	systems	at	regularly	scheduled	intervals. 272	|	P a g e
justification: Both AI opinions correctly identify that the state and configuration of Pod Security Policies (PSP) in Alibaba Cloud ACK clusters can be programmatically checked using Kubernetes APIs, which are accessible via Alibaba Cloud CLI, Aliyun CLI, or `kubectl`. The PSP resources and the admission controller status are exposed through these interfaces, making automation feasible and reliable for compliance auditing. Manual inspection is unnecessary given the robust API access.
automation_summary: - Alibaba Cloud Resource/Service: Alibaba Cloud Container Service for Kubernetes (ACK) Clusters - API/CLI Method: - Use `kubectl get psp` to list Pod Security Policies in the cluster. - Use `kubectl api-versions | grep policy/v1beta1` (or equivalent) to confirm PSP API is enabled. - Use Alibaba Cloud CLI or Aliyun CLI to fetch cluster configuration if available. - Properties to Check: - At least one PodSecurityPolicy resource exists (`kubectl get psp` returns non-empty list). - The PSP admission controller is enabled (PSP API version is present and active). - Policies are configured to restrict privileged containers and enforce namespace/workload security. - Pass Criteria: - PSP admission controller is enabled. - At least one PodSecurityPolicy is defined. - Policies enforce restrictions as per CIS recommendations (e.g., privileged: false, appropriate runAsUser, etc.). - Fail Criteria: - PSP admission controller is disabled. - No PodSecurityPolicies are defined. - Policies are overly permissive or missing required restrictions.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Consider ACK Sandboxed-Container for running untrusted workloads
description: Use	ACK	Sandboxed-Container	to	restrict	untrusted	workloads	as	an	additional	layer	of protection	when	running	in	a	multi-tenant	environment.
rationale: Sandboxed-Container	provides	an	alternative	to	the	Docker	runtime.	It	allows	you	to	run applications	in	a	sandboxed	and	lightweight	virtual	machine	that	has	a	dedicated	kernel. This	enhances	resource	isolation	and	improves	security.	Compared	with	Docker, SandboxedContainer	has	the	following	benefits: • Strong	isolation	based	on	sandboxed	and	lightweight	virtual	machines. • Network	Attached	Storage	(NAS)	file	systems	and	Alibaba	Cloud	disks	can	be mounted	to	Sandboxed-Container.	This	provides	the	same	storage	performance	as storage	volumes	that	are	mounted	to	the	host. • Compatibility	with	runC	in	terms	of	application	management. • High	performance	that	corresponds	to	90%	performance	of	applications	based	on runC. • The	same	user	experience	as	runC	in	terms	of	logging,	monitoring,	and	storage. • Support	for	RuntimeClass. • Easy	to	use	with	limited	expertise	that	is	required	to	use	virtual	machines. • Higher	stability	than	that	provided	by	Kata	Containers.
audit: Remediation: For	create	cluster: 1. Log	on	to	the	ACK	console	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/. 2. In	the	left-side	navigation	pane,	click	Clusters. 3. On	the	Clusters	page,	click	Create	Kubernetes	Cluster	in	the	upper-right	corner	of the	page. 4. On	the	Select	Cluster	Template	page,	find	Standard	Managed	Cluster	and	click	Create in	the	Standard	Managed	Cluster	section. 275	|	P a g e 5. On	the	ACK	managed	edition	tab,	configure	the	cluster	and	set	the	parameters	as required	in	the	following	list: • Zone:	Select	a	zone	where	Elastics	Compute	Service	(ECS)	Bare	Metal	instances	are deployed.	Sandboxed	containers	support	only	ECS	Bare	Metal	instances. • Kubernetes	Version:	Select	1.18.8-aliyun.1	or	later. • Container	Runtime:	Select	Sandboxed-Container. • Worker	Instance:	Add	worker	nodes	by	creating	new	ECS	instances. • Billing	Method:	Select	the	subscription	billing	method. • Instance	Type:	Select	ECS	Bare	Metal	instances. • Mount	Data	Disk:	Mount	a	data	disk	of	at	least	200	GiB.	We	recommend	that	you mount	a	data	disk	of	at	least	1	TB. • Operating	system:	By	default,	the	AliyunLinux	operating	system	is	used.	You	cannot change	the	operating	system. Default	Value: By	default,	Sandboxed	container	is	disabled References: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/142151.htm 2. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/140541.htm 3. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/160288.htm CIS	Controls: Version	7 18.9	Separate	Production	and	Non-Production	Systems Maintain	separate	environments	for	production	and	nonproduction	systems.	Developers should	not	have	unmonitored	access	to	production	environments. 276	|	P a g e

Developers should	not	have	unmonitored	access	to	production	environments. 276	|	P a g e
justification: While certain aspects of this control—such as verifying the container runtime for a cluster—can be automated using Alibaba Cloud CLI or API, the core requirement is to "consider" and apply ACK Sandboxed-Container specifically for "untrusted workloads." This involves subjective judgment about workload trust levels, business context, and security policy, which cannot be determined programmatically. There is no API or CLI method to automatically identify which workloads are "untrusted" or to confirm that Sandboxed-Container is being used for them according to organizational policy.
manual_summary: 1. **Access the ACK Console:** - Log in to the Alibaba Cloud ACK console at https://cs.console.aliyun.com/. - In the left navigation pane, click "Clusters." 2. **Review Cluster Configuration:** - Select the relevant Kubernetes cluster. - Check the "Container Runtime" setting to verify if "Sandboxed-Container" is enabled. - Confirm the cluster is running on ECS Bare Metal instances and is using Kubernetes version 1.18.8-aliyun.1 or later. 3. **Assess Workload Assignment:** - Review organizational security policies and documentation to determine which workloads are classified as "untrusted." - Cross-reference these workloads with the cluster(s) or node pools configured to use Sandboxed-Container. 4. **Confirm Compliance:** - Ensure that all untrusted workloads are deployed exclusively to clusters or node pools with Sandboxed-Container enabled. - Document evidence (screenshots, policy references, cluster settings) for audit purposes.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Consider ACK TEE-based when running confidential computing
description: ACK	provides	TEE-based	confidential	computing.	This	is	a	cloud-native	and	all-in-one solution	based	on	Intel	Software	Guard	Extensions	(SGX).	It	aims	to	deliver	and	manage trusted	applications	and	confidential	computing	applications	by	ensuring	the	security, integrity,	and	confidentiality	of	data	in	use.
rationale: ACK	TEE-based	confidential	computing	allows	you	to	isolate	sensitive	data	and	code	in	a trusted	execution	environment.	This	prevents	the	rest	part	of	the	system	from	accessing the	data.	Encrypted	data	in	a	trusted	execution	environment	is	unavailable	to	other applications,	the	BIOS,	the	operating	system,	the	kernel,	administrators,	O&M	personnel, cloud	vendors,	and	hardware	components	except	CPUs.	This	reduces	the	possibility	of sensitive	data	leaks	and	simplifies	data	management.
audit: Remediation: For	create	cluster: 1. Log	on	to	the	ACK	console	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/. 2. In	the	left-side	navigation	pane,	click	Clusters. 3. On	the	Clusters	page,	click	Create	Kubernetes	Cluster	in	the	upper-right	corner	of the page. 4. In	the	Select	Cluster	Template	dialog	box,	find	Confidential	Computing	Cluster	in	the Managed	Clusters	section	and	click	Create. 5. On	the	ACK	managed	edition	tab,	configure	the	cluster	and	set	the	parameters	as required in	the	following	list: • Zone:	Only	ECS	Bare	Metal	instances	of	the	ecs.ebmhfg5.2xlarge	type	support confidential	computing	clusters.	Make	sure	that	this	instance	type	is	available	in	the selected	zone. • Container	Runtime:	Docker	18.09.2. 277	|	P a g e • Instance	Type:	Select	ecs.ebmhfg5.2xlarge	of	the	ECS	Bare	Metal	instance	family. • Operating	System:	AliyunLinux	2.xxxx. • Network	Plug-in:	Flannel. • Custom	Image:	Do	not	use	custom	images.	Otherwise,	confidential	computing	may be	unavailable. Default	Value: By	default,	ACK	TEE-based	confidential	computing	is	disabled References: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/164536.htm 2. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/150518.htm 3. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/164537.htm CIS	Controls: Version	7 18.9	Separate	Production	and	Non-Production	Systems Maintain	separate	environments	for	production	and	nonproduction	systems.	Developers should	not	have	unmonitored	access	to	production	environments. 278	|	P a g e
references: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/164536.htm 2. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/150518.htm 3. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/164537.htm CIS	Controls: Version	7 18.9	Separate	Production	and	Non-Production	Systems Maintain	separate	environments	for	production	and	nonproduction	systems.	Developers should	not	have	unmonitored	access	to	production	environments. 278	|	P a g e
justification: While the first opinion leans toward manual verification, the second opinion correctly identifies that Alibaba Cloud exposes sufficient cluster configuration details via its APIs and CLI (e.g., `aliyun cs DescribeClusters` and `aliyun cs DescribeClusterDetail`). These APIs allow retrieval of cluster parameters, including whether a cluster is configured for confidential computing (TEE-based). Therefore, compliance can be programmatically checked by querying cluster properties and verifying the presence of confidential computing settings.
automation_summary: - Alibaba Cloud Resource/Service: Container Service for Kubernetes (ACK) Clusters - API/CLI Method: `aliyun cs DescribeClusters` and `aliyun cs DescribeClusterDetail` - Properties to Check: - For each cluster, check the `cluster_type` or `addons`/`features` fields for indicators such as `ConfidentialComputing`, `TEE`, or similar. - Confirm the presence of confidential computing/TEE-based configuration in the cluster specification. - Pass Criteria: - The cluster is explicitly marked as a confidential computing cluster (e.g., `ConfidentialComputing` or equivalent flag is enabled in the cluster details). - Fail Criteria: - The cluster does not have confidential computing/TEE-based settings enabled, or the relevant flag/feature is absent.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Consider use service account token volume projection
description: Use	service	account	token	volume	projection	to	enhance	security	when	you	use	service accounts.
rationale: The	feature	of	service	account	token	volume	projection	enables	the	kubelet	to	request	and store	the	token	on	behalf	of	the	pod,	and	allows	you	to	configure	token	properties	such	as the	audience	and	validity	duration.	As	the	token	approaches	expiration,	the	kubelet proactively	rotates	the	token	if	it	is	older	than	80	percent	of	its	time	to	live	(TTL)	or	if	it	is older	than	24	hours.
audit: Using	Command	Line: 1. Log	in	to	the	master	node	of	an	existing	dedicated	cluser	which	has	enabled	service account	token	volume	projection 2. Use	the	following	command	to	create	a	serviceaccount: kubectl apply -f - <<EOF apiVersion: v1 kind: ServiceAccount metadata: name: xxxxx EOF  3. Use	the	following	command	to	create	a	pod	which	using	the	projected serviceaccount token: kubectl apply -f - <<EOF apiVersion: v1 kind: Pod metadata: name: nginx spec: containers: 279	|	P a g e - image: nginx name: nginx volumeMounts: - mountPath: /var/run/secrets/tokens #customized volumemounts path for the token file name: vault-token serviceAccountName: xxxxx volumes: - name: vault-token projected: sources: - serviceAccountToken: path: vault-token  #customized audie expirationSeconds: 7200  #customized token expiration seconds audience: xxxxx    #customized audience which used to validate the tokens at the API server side  4. When	pod	running,	log	into	the	pod	and	make	sure	there	is	a	projected	token	file generated under	the	specific	path	defined	the	pod	spec	(e.g.	/var/run/secrets/tokens/vault- token	in the	sample	before).
remediation: For	create	cluster: 1. Log	on	to	the	ACK	console	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/. 2. In	the	left-side	navigation	pane,	click	Clusters. 3. On	the	Clusters	page,	click	Create	Kubernetes	Cluster	in	the	upper-right	corner	of the page. 4. On	the	ACK	managed	edition	tab,	click	the	Show	Advanced	Options,	and	select	the Enable check	box	to	enable	service	account	token	volume	projection. 5. Configure	the	the	parameters	of	service_account_issuer	and	api_audiences	upon	to the needed. Default	Value: By	default,	the	use	of	service	account	token	volume	projection	is	disabled References: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/160384.htm 280	|	P a g e 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/configure-service- account/ CIS	Controls: Version	7 4.3	Ensure	the	Use	of	Dedicated	Administrative	Accounts Ensure	that	all	users	with	administrative	account	access	use	a	dedicated	or	secondary account	for	elevated	activities.
